content.text:
Please analyze this codebase and create a CLAUDE.md file, which will be given to future instances of Claude Code to operate in this repository.

What to add:
1. Commands that will be commonly used, such as how to build, lint, and run tests. Include the necessary commands to develop in this codebase, such as how to run a single test.
2. High-level code architecture and structure so that future instances can be productive more quickly. Focus on the "big picture" architecture that requires reading multiple files to understand.

Usage notes:
- If there's already a CLAUDE.md, suggest improvements to it.
- When you make the initial CLAUDE.md, do not repeat yourself and do not include obvious instructions like "Provide helpful error messages to users", "Write unit tests for all new utilities", "Never include sensitive information (API keys, tokens) in code or commits".
- Avoid listing every component or file structure that can be easily discovered.
- Don't include generic development practices.
- If there are Cursor rules (in .cursor/rules/ or .cursorrules) or Copilot rules (in .github/copilot-instructions.md), make sure to include the important parts.
- If there is a README.md, make sure to include the important parts.
- Do not make up information such as "Common Development Tasks", "Tips for Development", "Support and Documentation" unless this is expressly included in other files that you read.
- Be sure to prefix the file with the following text:

```
# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.
```

attachment.content:
- docs-updater: Docs Updater Agent
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

input.prompt:
Design a plan to update the settings UI (`SwissKitJSettingUi.java`) to have a consistent glassmorphism (液态玻璃) look matching the main application.

## Background

The project is a JavaFX application with a dark theme + glassmorphism design system. The CSS is at `SwissKit/src/main/resources/css/glass.css`. The main window uses:
- Semi-transparent backgrounds (`rgba(13,14,17,0.72)` for the main window pane)
- Gaussian blur orbs behind the window for depth
- Border with `rgba(255,255,255,0.10)` for glass edges
- Color palette: accent=`#5b8cf7`, backgrounds at various opacities (`0.022`, `0.055`, `0.085`), text at `0.92`/`0.50`/`0.28` alpha
- `glass.css` defines many CSS classes but the settings UI currently uses inline styles instead of these classes

## Current State

`SwissKitJSettingUi.java` (1025 lines) has:
1. **Main container** — already has a glass border style inline: `rgba(255,255,255,0.022)` bg, `rgba(255,255,255,0.10)` border, 12px radius
2. **TabPane with 3 tabs** — custom inline styles for tab header, active/inactive tab styling with `#5b8cf7` accent
3. **3 dialog windows** (opened via `Stage`):
   - Address Book dialog — uses `#1c1f26` solid dark bg (line 465)
   - Add/Edit Address dialog — uses `#1c1f26` solid dark bg (line 599)
   - Manage Tags dialog — uses `#1c1f26` solid dark bg (line 708)
4. **UI helper methods** at bottom — `fieldStyle()`, `comboStyle()`, `glassBtn()`, `labeled()`, `sectionTitle()` produce inline styles that somewhat match the glass theme
5. All dialog scenes load `glass.css` stylesheet (lines 470, 604, 713)

## What's Needed

The user wants ALL of the settings UI (main container + dialog windows) to match the main program's glassmorphism liquid glass style. The key changes are:

1. **Dialog windows**: Replace `#1c1f26` solid background with glassmorphism — semi-transparent dark background with a frosted glass border effect
2. **Overall consistency**: Ensure all inline styles use the same color palette and opacity levels as glass.css
3. **Where possible**: Use CSS style classes from `glass.css` instead of inline styles (but JavaFX CSS 2.1 subset limitations mean some things must stay inline)

## Constraints
- The dialog windows are separate `Stage` instances, so they can't inherit styles from the main scene root
- JavaFX CSS has limited support for looked-up colors across separate stages
- The settings page itself is embedded in the main ContentArea (via `MainWindow.openSettings()`), so it's already inside the main glass window
- The helper methods (`fieldStyle()`, `glassBtn()`, etc.) are used throughout — changes there propagate everywhere

## Design Requirements
- Dialog backgrounds should look like frosted glass floating panels
- Use `rgba(13,14,17,0.78)` or similar for dialog background (matching main window depth)
- Add glass border effect: `rgba(255,255,255,0.10)` border with rounded corners
- Add drop shadow to dialogs for depth
- Text fields, buttons, combos should use consistent semi-transparent styling
- Scrollable content should have the thin glass scrollbar styling from glass.css
- Active/selected states should use the `#5b8cf7` accent consistently

Please produce a detailed implementation plan with specific line numbers and code changes needed.

content.thinking:
The Plan agent has provided a comprehensive plan. Let me now review it and write the final plan to the plan file. Let me also think about whether there are any issues with the plan:

1. **scene.setFill(Color.TRANSPARENT)** for dialogs — this is risky. Dialog stages on macOS/Windows/Linux have OS window chrome (title bar). Setting TRANSPARENT fill on a decorated window may not work correctly or may look weird. The dialogs here use `Stage.initModality(Modality.APPLICATION_MODAL)` but don't set `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`, so they have OS decorations. Setting TRANSPARENT fill with OS decorations could cause rendering artifacts. Let me not include this change — instead the glass-dialog background will work fine with the default scene fill since the background has rounded corners and drop shadow via CSS.

Actually, looking at the existing code more carefully:
- The main app uses `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT` (mentioned in CLAUDE.md)
- But the dialog stages in the settings UI don't specify a style, so they get `StageStyle.DECORATED` by default

For decorated dialogs, `scene.setFill(Color.TRANSPARENT)` would make the area behind the rounded corners transparent, showing the OS window background (usually white/gray). That won't look good.

Better approach: Don't set TRANSPARENT fill on dialogs. Instead, just use the glass-dialog class with its semi-transparent background and rounded corners. The solid scene fill behind it won't matter because the VBox fills the scene.

Actually wait — if the scene fill is the default (white or whatever the OS provides), and the VBox has `glass-dialog` with `rgba(13,14,17,0.78)` + rounded corners, the corners will show the scene fill color. That won't look like glass — the corners will be a solid color.

The best approach for dialogs:
1. Keep the scene with default fill
2. Set the root VBox background to `rgba(13,14,17,0.85)` (more opaque to hide the scene fill, since there's no orb layer)
3. Add glass border
4. The drop shadow will help with depth

Actually, the cleaner approach is:
- Set scene fill to `#0d0e11` (the same dark color as the app background) 
- Then use the glass-dialog on the root VBox with the semi-transparent overlay

This way the dialog has a dark background, and the glass panel floats on top of it with the border and shadow effect.

Let me simplify the plan and write it up. The key changes are:

1. Add CSS classes to glass.css
2. Update helper methods to use CSS classes
3. Fix dialog backgrounds from #1c1f26 to glassmorphism
4. Minor polish on the main container

Let me write the final plan file now.

Now let me write the final plan file.

Key decisions:
1. Don't use `scene.setFill(Color.TRANSPARENT)` for dialogs — they're decorated stages. Instead, set scene fill to the app's dark background color (`#0d0e11`) so the glass-dialog background blends properly.
2. The glass-dialog for separate stages should be more opaque than the main window since there's no orb layer behind them.
3. Keep the `fieldStyle()` method but make it apply CSS class instead of inline style.
4. The tab header styling is specific to this component and works well enough inline — focus on the dialogs and helper methods.

input.content:
# Plan: Settings UI Glassmorphism Consistency

## Context

The settings UI (`SwissKitJSettingUi.java`) has inline glass-like styles but the three dialog windows use solid `#1c1f26` backgrounds that clash with the main app's liquid glass design. The goal is to make every part of the settings UI match the glassmorphism aesthetic from `glass.css`.

## Approach

Add CSS classes to `glass.css`, then update the Java code to use them — replacing inline styles and the solid dialog backgrounds with glassmorphism.

---

## Step 1: Add CSS classes to `glass.css`

Append these classes after the `.section-title` block (after line 396):

- **`.glass-dialog`** — frosted glass panel for dialog `Stage` roots: `rgba(13,14,17,0.85)` bg, `rgba(255,255,255,0.10)` border, 12px radius, drop shadow. More opaque than the main window pane since dialogs have no orb layer behind them.
- **`.glass-field` + `.glass-field:focused`** — form inputs with accent glow on focus (matches search-bar pattern)
- **`.glass-field-label`** — 11px/`0.50` alpha label for form field labels
- **`.section-header`** — 15px/`0.92` alpha for larger section titles (distinct from existing `.section-title` at `0.28`)
- **`.glass-combo`** — ComboBox glass styling
- **`.glass-table`** — TableView with glass header, accent-colored selection rows, hover states, transparent placeholder
- **`.glass-checkbox`** — CheckBox with glass box styling and accent selected state
- **`.glass-btn-primary`** — accent-filled button with hover glow + lift
- **`.glass-btn-secondary`** — ghost button with hover state

## Step 2: Update UI helper methods

In `SwissKitJSettingUi.java` lines 937-1016, update the factory methods to use CSS classes:

1. **`glassBtn()`** (line 992) — use `glass-btn-primary` / `glass-btn-secondary` classes instead of inline strings
2. **`fieldStyle()`** (line 977) — use `glass-field` class
3. **`comboStyle()`** (line 985) — use `glass-combo` class
4. **`sectionTitle()`** (line 952) — use `section-header` class
5. **`subLabel()`** (line 958) — use `glass-field-label` class
6. **`labeled()`** (line 941) — use `glass-field-label` class for the label
7. **`textField()`** (line 964) — apply `glass-field` class when no custom style
8. **`passwordField()`** (line 971) — apply `glass-field` class

## Step 3: Fix dialog windows (core change)

Each of the 3 dialogs needs its root VBox background changed and scene fill set to dark:

### Address Book dialog (line ~403)
- Line 465: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: #1c1f26;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-dialog")`
- Line 418: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: transparent;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-table")` on the TableView
- After scene creation: set scene fill to `#0d0e11` so glass-dialog blends

### Add/Edit Address dialog (line ~476)
- Line 599: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: #1c1f26;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-dialog")`
- After scene creation: set scene fill to `#0d0e11`
- Text fields and tagCombo automatically benefit from Step 2 helper changes

### Manage Tags dialog (line ~610)
- Line 708: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: #1c1f26;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-dialog")`
- Line 625: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: transparent;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-table")`
- After scene creation: set scene fill to `#0d0e11`

## Step 4: Polish main settings container

- Line 72-79: add `glass-dialog` class, simplify inline style
- Line 729: plugin TableView — use `glass-table` class
- Lines 293-297: TLS/SSL checkboxes — use `glass-checkbox` class

## Step 5: Build verification

Run Maven build to verify no compilation errors:
```bash
mvn compile -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
```

If the build succeeds, the changes are structurally correct.

---

## Files modified

1. `SwissKit/src/main/resources/css/glass.css` — append 8 new CSS class blocks
2. `SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/ui/setting/SwissKitJSettingUi.java` — update helpers (lines 937-1016), dialogs (lines 403-717), container (lines 70-143)

content.text:
All done. Here's a summary of what changed:

### `glass.css` — 8 new CSS class blocks
- **`.glass-dialog`** — frosted glass panel for dialog windows (`rgba(13,14,17,0.85)` + border + drop shadow)
- **`.glass-field` + `:focused`** — form inputs with accent glow on focus
- **`.glass-field-label`** — 11px semi-transparent form labels
- **`.section-header`** — 15px bold title variant
- **`.glass-combo`** — ComboBox glass styling
- **`.glass-table`** — TableView with glass header, accent selection, hover rows
- **`.glass-checkbox`** — CheckBox with glass box and accent selected state
- **`.glass-btn-primary` / `.glass-btn-secondary`** — buttons with hover/pressed effects

### `SwissKitJSettingUi.java` — 15 edits
- **Helper methods** (8): `glassBtn()`, `fieldStyle()`, `comboStyle()`, `sectionTitle()`, `subLabel()`, `labeled()`, `textField()`, `passwordField()` all now use CSS classes instead of inline strings. Removed dead `fieldStyle()`/`comboStyle()` methods.
- **3 dialog windows**: replaced solid `#1c1f26` backgrounds with `glass-dialog` class + `#0d0e11` scene fill, TableViews use `glass-table`
- **Main container**: plugin TableView → `glass-table`, TLS/SSL checkboxes → `glass-checkbox`, ComboBoxes → `glass-combo`

content.text: tabe页看不清楚，标签使用圆角，选中是页现实圆角

content.thinking: The user is saying that the tab headers are not clear/hard to see, and they want the tabs to use rounded corners, with the selected tab showing rounded corners. Let me look at the current tab styling in the settings UI to understand what needs to change.

content.text: Let me look at the current tab styling.

content.thinking:
The user's complaint is:
1. The tab headers are hard to see (看不清楚)
2. They want rounded corners (圆角) on the tabs
3. The selected tab should show rounded corners

Currently the tabs have:
- Inline styles with transparent background
- Bottom border of 2px for the selected tab indicator (using accent color)
- No rounded corners (no `-fx-background-radius` or `-fx-border-radius`)

The current approach uses `t.setStyle()` which sets styles on the Tab itself. However, JavaFX Tab styling is tricky — the actual visual tab header is rendered by the TabPane skin, not directly by the Tab node. Setting `-fx-background-radius` on the Tab via `setStyle()` may or may not work depending on the JavaFX version.

For JavaFX TabPane, the best approach is:
1. Add CSS classes for tab styling in the TabPane's stylesheet
2. Use `-fx-background-radius` and `-fx-border-radius` on the tab header area

In JavaFX, the tab header area can be styled via CSS selectors like:
- `.tab-pane .tab-header-area .tab-header-background` — background of the header area
- `.tab-pane .tab` — individual tab
- `.tab-pane .tab:selected` — selected tab
- `.tab-pane .tab .tab-label` — tab text label

But the current code sets styles on Tab nodes directly using `setStyle()`, which applies inline styles. Inline styles override CSS classes. The current approach uses a `VBox tabHeader` wrapper around the TabPane, which is unusual.

Let me redesign this:
1. Remove the VBox wrapper around TabPane
2. Set CSS classes on the TabPane for glassmorphism styling
3. Use a glass.css class to style the tab headers with rounded corners
4. Selected tab should have a distinct rounded background with accent color

Let me add proper TabPane CSS to glass.css and then update the Java code.

For JavaFX TabPane styling, the key CSS selectors are:
```css
.glass-tab-pane .tab-header-area .tab-header-background {
    /* background of entire tab header area */
}
.glass-tab-pane .tab-header-area .headers-region .tab {
```

content.thinking:
Now I see the pattern. In `ExcelSplitterPlugin.createView()`:
- A `VBox` root wraps the content
- `VBox.setVgrow(wizard, Priority.ALWAYS)` makes the wizard fill vertical space
- `root.setPadding(new Insets(24))` for padding
- `root.setStyle("-fx-background-color: transparent;")` for transparent background
- The root VBox doesn't set a fixed width — it naturally expands to fill the ScrollPane viewport

The key difference with the settings UI:
1. The settings container has explicit border/background styling that might constrain it
2. The container VBox wraps an HBox (sidebar + content), but the VBox itself needs to fill the available space
3. The sidebar has fixed width, and the content StackPane needs to fill remaining width via `HBox.setHgrow`

The issue is that the settings container `VBox` doesn't grow to fill the available height. When inside a `ScrollPane`, the content should fill the viewport. Let me check the current code again.

Looking at the settings build() method, the container VBox has:
```java
VBox container = new VBox(body);
container.setStyle(...);
VBox.setVgrow(body, Priority.ALWAYS);
```

The `VBox.setVgrow(body, Priority.ALWAYS)` ensures the body fills the VBox height. But the VBox itself needs to fill the ScrollPane viewport.

The issue is that ScrollPane with `fitToWidth=true` only constrains width, not height. For height, a `VBox` uses its preferred height (sum of children). With `VBox.setVgrow(body, Priority.ALWAYS)`, the body will be allocated whatever height the VBox has.

But the VBox's preferred height is determined by its content. If the sidebar is 180px wide, it'll have a natural height based on its children (section label + 3 nav items + spacer). The spacer with `VBox.setVgrow(spacer, Priority.ALWAYS)` means it'll take up all remaining height.

But where does that remaining height come from? The VBox container needs to be given a height by its parent (the ScrollPane). With `fitToWidth=true`, ScrollPane sizes content width to viewport. But what about height?

Looking at the ScrollPane setup in ContentArea:
```java
private ScrollPane buildPageScrollPane() {
    ScrollPane sp = new ScrollPane();
    sp.getStyleClass().add("content-scroll");
    sp.setFitToWidth(true);
    sp.setHbarPolicy(ScrollPane.ScrollBarPolicy.NEVER);
    sp.setVbarPolicy(ScrollPane.ScrollBarPolicy.AS_NEEDED);
    sp.setStyle("-fx-background-color: transparent; -fx-background: transparent;");
    return sp;
}
```

`setFitToHeight` is not set (defaults to false). So the ScrollPane only fits width, not height. The content's height is its preferred height, and if it exceeds the viewport, scrollbars appear.

The ExcelSplitterPlugin view fills naturally because:

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Run

**Build order is required** — the API module must be installed before anything else compiles:

```bash
# Step 1: Install the API module
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Step 2: Build the full project (from repo root)
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run the application
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

To build and run only the main app module:
```bash
mvn clean package -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
```

On Windows, the `windows-exe` Maven profile is auto-activated and produces `SwissKit.exe` via Launch4j. GitHub Actions handles multi-platform builds — local Maven is not required for releases.

## Module Structure

| Module | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared plugin interface + reusable UI components (`SwissKitJPlugin`, `StepWizard`) |
| `SwissKit` | Main JavaFX application — UI shell, plugin loading, built-in tools |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning` | Auto-learning plugin |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc` | CSV-to-Excel converter plugin |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKit-Plugin-Mouse` | Mouse automation plugin |

All plugins declare `SwissKitJ-Api` as `provided` scope. The main app provides it at runtime via the fat JAR.

## Architecture

**Entry point**: `fan.summer.Launcher` (fat-JAR manifest) → `fan.summer.app.SwissKitJApp` (JavaFX `Application`).

**Startup sequence** (in `SwissKitJApp.start()`):
1. Resolve `plugins/` directory (JAR sibling in production, `./plugins/` in dev)
2. Create `PluginLoader` + `PluginRegistry`
3. Register built-in tools via `BuiltinToolRegistrar` (bypasses JAR loading, directly adds to registry)
4. Build `MainWindow` and display it
5. Start `PluginLoader` (scans `plugins/` dir and watches for changes)

**UI structure** (all in `fan.summer.ui.*`):
- `MainWindow` — root `StackPane`; owns `TitleBar`, `Sidebar`, `ContentArea`, status bar
- `Sidebar` — category-based navigation; categories are `all / text / image / dev / net / other`
- `ContentArea` — shows `ToolCard` grid or active tool view; manages `DetailPanel` and the back-bar for returning from a tool
- `DetailPanel` — slide-in panel showing plugin metadata; has a Launch button that fires `onLaunch`
- `TitleBar` — custom window chrome (window is `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`)

**Navigation flow**: `ToolCard` click → `DetailPanel.show()` → Launch button → `MainWindow.wireEvents` callback → `registry.activate(plugin)` + `contentArea.showPage(plugin.createView(), title)`. The back bar (shown by `ContentArea`) calls `registry.deactivate()` on return.

**Theming**: Single CSS file at `src/main/resources/css/glass.css` (glassmorphism). Plugin icon background colors are CSS classes: `ic-blue / ic-purple / ic-teal / ic-amber / ic-red / ic-pink / ic-gray`.

**Database**: H2 file at `.swisskit/swisskit.db` relative to the runtime working directory. Schema initialized from `init.sql`. Accessed via MyBatis; mapper XMLs are in `src/main/resources/mapper/`.

**i18n**: `src/main/resources/i18n/messages.properties` (Chinese default), `messages_en.properties` (English).

## Reusable UI Component: StepWizard

`fan.summer.api.component.StepWizard` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`) is a ready-made multi-step wizard container for use inside any plugin's `createView()`.

```java
StepWizard wizard = new StepWizard();
wizard.addStep("Select file",  step1Node, () -> filePath != null);
wizard.addStep("Split mode",   step2Node, () -> modeSelected);
wizard.addStep("Output path",  step3Node, () -> outputPath != null);
wizard.build();   // must call after all addStep() calls

// Intercept step transitions (e.g., trigger async work when moving from step 0 → 1):
wizard.setOnStepChanged((from, to, total) -> {
    if (from == 0 && to == 1) startAnalysis();
});

// Programmatic navigation:
wizard.goTo(2);
boolean last = wizard.isLastStep();
```

The wizard renders step dots with done/active/idle states, animated slide transitions between steps, and Back/Next buttons. The `canProceed` supplier is evaluated on every Next click — return `false` to trigger a shake animation and block advancement.

## Plugin Development

**Interface**: `fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`)

```java
public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
    String getId();          // reverse-domain ID, e.g. "com.example.my-tool"
    String getName();
    String getDescription();
    String getCategory();    // dev / text / image / net / other
    String getVersion();
    String getIconText();    // emoji or single char
    default String getIconStyle() { return "ic-blue"; }  // CSS class for icon bg
    default String getType()      { return "plugin"; }   // "builtin" for built-ins

    Node createView();       // called once; result cached and reused
    default void onActivate()   {}
    default void onDeactivate() {}
    default void onUnload()     {}
}
```

**External plugins** (JAR-based):
1. Implement `SwissKitJPlugin`
2. Declare in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin`
3. Drop JAR into `plugins/` directory; hot-reload is supported

**Built-in tools** skip SPI entirely — `BuiltinToolRegistrar.register()` adds them directly to `PluginRegistry`. See existing tools there as templates.

### Plugin logging

Plugins should use `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`) rather than depending on SLF4J directly. The host installs a binder at startup that routes plugin log calls into the same SLF4J + Logback backbone used by the host (console at INFO+, rolling file at DEBUG+ under `.swisskit/logs/swisskit.log`, daily rotation, 7-day retention).

```java
import fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory;
import fan.summer.api.log.PluginLogger;

public class MyPlugin implements SwissKitJPlugin {
    private static final PluginLogger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyPlugin.class);

    @Override public void onActivate() {
        log.info("Activated, taskId={}", currentTaskId);
    }
}
```

Use SLF4J-style `{}` placeholders — formatting is deferred until the level is actually enabled. If the host has not installed a binder (e.g. plugin unit tests), `LoggerFactory` returns a silent no-op logger, so it is safe to call from anywhere.

## Branch Status — v3.0.0-JavaFX

This branch is an active migration from Swing/FlatLaf to JavaFX. Legacy Swing classes live in `SwissKit/backup/` and are **excluded from Maven compilation** via `<excludes>` in `SwissKit/pom.xml`. Do not move files out of `backup/` unless completing their JavaFX port.

The plugin interface was also renamed: the old `fan.summer.api.KitPage` (Swing `JPanel`-based) is replaced by `fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` (JavaFX `Node`-based).

## Excel Splitter — Porting Reference

The backup Swing implementation at `SwissKit/backup/java/fan/summer/kitpage/excel/` is the authoritative reference for the Excel split logic. Key classes:

| Backup class | Role |
|---|---|
| `ExcelKitPage` | Top-level Swing page (UI only — replace with JavaFX + StepWizard) |
| `ExcelAnalysisWorker` | Reads all sheets + row-0 headers via Apache POI → `Map<String, Map<Integer, String>>` |
| `ExcelSplitWorker` | Three split modes — see below |
| `NoModelDataListener` | Apache Fesod `AnalysisEventListener` that caches rows as `List<Map<Integer,Object>>` |
| `ExcelUtil` | POI helpers: `appendSheet`, `appendDataRowsByPoi`, `copyEntireSheet`, `normalizeOrInvalid` |
| `FileNameUtil` | `getFileName(String)` — strips extension |

**Three split modes** (set via `ExcelSplitWorker.setXxxModel()`):

| Mode key | Method | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `SSM` | `setSplitSheetModel(Set<String> sheets)` | One output file per selected sheet |
| `SCM` | `setSplitColumnModel(String sheet, String column)` | Groups rows by unique column value → one file per value |
| `SCPM` | `setComplexSplitModel(String taskId)` | Multi-config: reads `ComplexSplitConfigEntity` rows from H2; supports normal split + copy-all (headerIndex==-1 && columnIndex==-1) |

**Analysis result map shape**: `Map<sheetName, Map<columnIndex, columnHeader>>` — the outer key is sheet name (insertion order preserved via `LinkedHashMap`), the inner key is the zero-based column index from POI.

**Fesod library** (`org.apache.fesod:fesod-sheet`) is used for high-throughput reading/writing in split operations. Core pattern:
```java
// Read
NoModelDataListener listener = new NoModelDataListener();
try (ExcelReader reader = FesodSheet.read(file).build()) {
    ReadSheet sheet = FesodSheet.readSheet(sheetName)
        .headRowNumber(headerRowIndex)
        .registerReadListener(listener).build();
    reader.read(sheet);
}
List<Map<Integer, Object>> rows = listener.getCachedDataList();

// Write
FesodSheet.write(outputFile)
    .sheet(sheetName)
    .head(buildHeaders(headerMap))   // List<List<String>>
    .doWrite(buildRows(headerMap, rows));  // List<List<Object>>
```

**Complex split DB entity**: `ComplexSplitConfigEntity` fields — `taskId`, `fieldName` (original filename), `sheetName`, `headerIndex` (1-based row of headers), `columnIndex` (1-based column to split by). A row with `headerIndex == -1 && columnIndex == -1` means "copy entire sheet to all output files".

## Custom Commands

- `/docs-updater` — Updates version numbers and content across `docs/`, `README.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` to match current pom.xml versions.
- `/release <version>` — Full release workflow: bumps all pom.xml versions, updates `CHANGELOG.md`, commits, creates and pushes a `v{version}` git tag, which triggers GitHub Actions for multi-platform builds.

## Commit Convention

Follow conventional commits with emojis:
- `✨` / `:sparkles:` — new feature
- `🐛` / `:bug:` — bug fix
- `♻️` / `:recycle:` — refactor
- `📝` / `:memo:` — documentation

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Build & Run

**Build order is required** — the API module must be installed before anything else compiles:

```bash
# Step 1: Install the API module
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Step 2: Build the full project (from repo root)
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run the application
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

To build and run only the main app module:
```bash
mvn clean package -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
```

On Windows, the `windows-exe` Maven profile is auto-activated and produces `SwissKit.exe` via Launch4j. GitHub Actions handles multi-platform builds — local Maven is not required for releases.

## Module Structure

| Module | Purpose |
|--------|---------|
| `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared plugin interface + reusable UI components (`SwissKitJPlugin`, `StepWizard`) |
| `SwissKit` | Main JavaFX application — UI shell, plugin loading, built-in tools |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning` | Auto-learning plugin |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc` | CSV-to-Excel converter plugin |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKit-Plugin-Mouse` | Mouse automation plugin |

All plugins declare `SwissKitJ-Api` as `provided` scope. The main app provides it at runtime via the fat JAR.

## Architecture

**Entry point**: `fan.summer.Launcher` (fat-JAR manifest) → `fan.summer.app.SwissKitJApp` (JavaFX `Application`).

**Startup sequence** (in `SwissKitJApp.start()`):
1. Resolve `plugins/` directory (JAR sibling in production, `./plugins/` in dev)
2. Create `PluginLoader` + `PluginRegistry`
3. Register built-in tools via `BuiltinToolRegistrar` (bypasses JAR loading, directly adds to registry)
4. Build `MainWindow` and display it
5. Start `PluginLoader` (scans `plugins/` dir and watches for changes)

**UI structure** (all in `fan.summer.ui.*`):
- `MainWindow` — root `StackPane`; owns `TitleBar`, `Sidebar`, `ContentArea`, status bar
- `Sidebar` — category-based navigation; categories are `all / text / image / dev / net / other`
- `ContentArea` — shows `ToolCard` grid or active tool view; manages `DetailPanel` and the back-bar for returning from a tool
- `DetailPanel` — slide-in panel showing plugin metadata; has a Launch button that fires `onLaunch`
- `TitleBar` — custom window chrome (window is `StageStyle.TRANSPARENT`)

**Navigation flow**: `ToolCard` click → `DetailPanel.show()` → Launch button → `MainWindow.wireEvents` callback → `registry.activate(plugin)` + `contentArea.showPage(plugin.createView(), title)`. The back bar (shown by `ContentArea`) calls `registry.deactivate()` on return.

**Theming**: Single CSS file at `src/main/resources/css/glass.css` (glassmorphism). Plugin icon background colors are CSS classes: `ic-blue / ic-purple / ic-teal / ic-amber / ic-red / ic-pink / ic-gray`.

**Database**: H2 file at `.swisskit/swisskit.db` relative to the runtime working directory. Schema initialized from `init.sql`. Accessed via MyBatis; mapper XMLs are in `src/main/resources/mapper/`.

**i18n**: `src/main/resources/i18n/messages.properties` (Chinese default), `messages_en.properties` (English).

## Reusable UI Component: StepWizard

`fan.summer.api.component.StepWizard` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`) is a ready-made multi-step wizard container for use inside any plugin's `createView()`.

```java
StepWizard wizard = new StepWizard();
wizard.addStep("Select file",  step1Node, () -> filePath != null);
wizard.addStep("Split mode",   step2Node, () -> modeSelected);
wizard.addStep("Output path",  step3Node, () -> outputPath != null);
wizard.build();   // must call after all addStep() calls

// Intercept step transitions (e.g., trigger async work when moving from step 0 → 1):
wizard.setOnStepChanged((from, to, total) -> {
    if (from == 0 && to == 1) startAnalysis();
});

// Programmatic navigation:
wizard.goTo(2);
boolean last = wizard.isLastStep();
```

The wizard renders step dots with done/active/idle states, animated slide transitions between steps, and Back/Next buttons. The `canProceed` supplier is evaluated on every Next click — return `false` to trigger a shake animation and block advancement.

## Plugin Development

**Interface**: `fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`)

```java
public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
    String getId();          // reverse-domain ID, e.g. "com.example.my-tool"
    String getName();
    String getDescription();
    String getCategory();    // dev / text / image / net / other
    String getVersion();
    String getIconText();    // emoji or single char
    default String getIconStyle() { return "ic-blue"; }  // CSS class for icon bg
    default String getType()      { return "plugin"; }   // "builtin" for built-ins

    Node createView();       // called once; result cached and reused
    default void onActivate()   {}
    default void onDeactivate() {}
    default void onUnload()     {}
}
```

**External plugins** (JAR-based):
1. Implement `SwissKitJPlugin`
2. Declare in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin`
3. Drop JAR into `plugins/` directory; hot-reload is supported

**Built-in tools** skip SPI entirely — `BuiltinToolRegistrar.register()` adds them directly to `PluginRegistry`. See existing tools there as templates.

### Plugin logging

Plugins should use `fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory` (in `SwissKitJ-Api`) rather than depending on SLF4J directly. The host installs a binder at startup that routes plugin log calls into the same SLF4J + Logback backbone used by the host (console at INFO+, rolling file at DEBUG+ under `.swisskit/logs/swisskit.log`, daily rotation, 7-day retention).

```java
import fan.summer.api.log.LoggerFactory;
import fan.summer.api.log.PluginLogger;

public class MyPlugin implements SwissKitJPlugin {
    private static final PluginLogger log = LoggerFactory.getLogger(MyPlugin.class);

    @Override public void onActivate() {
        log.info("Activated, taskId={}", currentTaskId);
    }
}
```

Use SLF4J-style `{}` placeholders — formatting is deferred until the level is actually enabled. If the host has not installed a binder (e.g. plugin unit tests), `LoggerFactory` returns a silent no-op logger, so it is safe to call from anywhere.

## Branch Status — v3.0.0-JavaFX

This branch is an active migration from Swing/FlatLaf to JavaFX. Legacy Swing classes live in `backup/SwissKit/` and `backup/SwissKitJ-Api/` under the project root, and are **excluded from Maven compilation** via `<excludes>` in `SwissKit/pom.xml`. Do not move files out of `backup/` unless completing their JavaFX port.

The plugin interface was also renamed: the old `fan.summer.api.KitPage` (Swing `JPanel`-based) is replaced by `fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` (JavaFX `Node`-based).

## Excel Splitter — Porting Reference

The backup Swing implementation at `SwissKit/backup/java/fan/summer/kitpage/excel/` is the authoritative reference for the Excel split logic. Key classes:

| Backup class | Role |
|---|---|
| `ExcelKitPage` | Top-level Swing page (UI only — replace with JavaFX + StepWizard) |
| `ExcelAnalysisWorker` | Reads all sheets + row-0 headers via Apache POI → `Map<String, Map<Integer, String>>` |
| `ExcelSplitWorker` | Three split modes — see below |
| `NoModelDataListener` | Apache Fesod `AnalysisEventListener` that caches rows as `List<Map<Integer,Object>>` |
| `ExcelUtil` | POI helpers: `appendSheet`, `appendDataRowsByPoi`, `copyEntireSheet`, `normalizeOrInvalid` |
| `FileNameUtil` | `getFileName(String)` — strips extension |

**Three split modes** (set via `ExcelSplitWorker.setXxxModel()`):

| Mode key | Method | What it does |
|---|---|---|
| `SSM` | `setSplitSheetModel(Set<String> sheets)` | One output file per selected sheet |
| `SCM` | `setSplitColumnModel(String sheet, String column)` | Groups rows by unique column value → one file per value |
| `SCPM` | `setComplexSplitModel(String taskId)` | Multi-config: reads `ComplexSplitConfigEntity` rows from H2; supports normal split + copy-all (headerIndex==-1 && columnIndex==-1) |

**Analysis result map shape**: `Map<sheetName, Map<columnIndex, columnHeader>>` — the outer key is sheet name (insertion order preserved via `LinkedHashMap`), the inner key is the zero-based column index from POI.

**Fesod library** (`org.apache.fesod:fesod-sheet`) is used for high-throughput reading/writing in split operations. Core pattern:
```java
// Read
NoModelDataListener listener = new NoModelDataListener();
try (ExcelReader reader = FesodSheet.read(file).build()) {
    ReadSheet sheet = FesodSheet.readSheet(sheetName)
        .headRowNumber(headerRowIndex)
        .registerReadListener(listener).build();
    reader.read(sheet);
}
List<Map<Integer, Object>> rows = listener.getCachedDataList();

// Write
FesodSheet.write(outputFile)
    .sheet(sheetName)
    .head(buildHeaders(headerMap))   // List<List<String>>
    .doWrite(buildRows(headerMap, rows));  // List<List<Object>>
```

**Complex split DB entity**: `ComplexSplitConfigEntity` fields — `taskId`, `fieldName` (original filename), `sheetName`, `headerIndex` (1-based row of headers), `columnIndex` (1-based column to split by). A row with `headerIndex == -1 && columnIndex == -1` means "copy entire sheet to all output files".

## Custom Commands

- `/docs-updater` — Updates version numbers and content across `docs/`, `README.md`, and `CHANGELOG.md` to match current pom.xml versions.
- `/release <version>` — Full release workflow: bumps all pom.xml versions, updates `CHANGELOG.md`, commits, creates and pushes a `v{version}` git tag, which triggers GitHub Actions for multi-platform builds.

## Commit Convention

Follow conventional commits with emojis:
- `✨` / `:sparkles:` — new feature
- `🐛` / `:bug:` — bug fix
- `♻️` / `:recycle:` — refactor
- `📝` / `:memo:` — documentation
- `⬆️` / `:arrow_up:` — dependency upgrade

content.thinking:
The issue is that the `desc` label, despite having `setWrapText(true)`, is being constrained by the VBox layout. The VBox (`OnlineStorePane extends VBox`) is inside a `ScrollPane` in `PluginStoreUi`, which has `fitToWidth=true` and `fitToHeight=true`. 

The `ScrollPane` constrains the content to viewport width, so the VBox gets the full width. But the `Label` might not be getting its preferred width because the label's max width is unbounded and with wrapText=true it should wrap to the available width.

Wait, actually the issue might be that the label doesn't have its max width constrained to the parent's width. In JavaFX, a `Label` with `wrapText=true` will wrap only if its max width is constrained. Without a max width, it tries to use its preferred width (single-line).

The fix is to either:
1. Set `desc.setMaxWidth(Double.MAX_VALUE)` so it takes the full width
2. Or bind the label's max width to the parent's width

Let me add `desc.setMaxWidth(Double.MAX_VALUE)`.

The issue is likely that the label's preferred height is calculated incorrectly because the Label doesn't know its wrap width during layout. The fix might be to:

Actually the best approach for JavaFX is:
```java
desc.wrappingWidthProperty().bind(
    Bindings.createDoubleBinding(
        () -> getWidth() - getPadding().getLeft() - getPadding().getRight(),
        widthProperty(), paddingProperty()
    )
);
```

This binds the label's wrap width to the parent VBox's actual width minus padding. This ensures proper multi-line wrapping.

1	name: Release
2	
3	on:
4	  workflow_dispatch:
5	    inputs:
6	      tag:
7	        description: 'Tag to release (e.g. v1.2.3 or v1.0.0-beta.1)'
8	        required: true
9	        type: string
10	  push:
11	    tags:
12	      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+'
13	      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-alpha\.[0-9]+'
14	      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-beta\.[0-9]+'
15	      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-rc\.[0-9]+'
16	
17	permissions:
18	  contents: write
19	
20	jobs:
21	  # -------------------------------------------------
22	  # Resolve version strings once; all build jobs use
23	  # these outputs so there is a single source of truth.
24	  #
25	  # RAW_TAG  : original tag, e.g. v2.1.0-beta.1
26	  # VERSION  : semver digits only, e.g. 2.1.0   (used as Maven project.version)
27	  # FULL_TAG : tag without leading 'v', e.g. 2.1.0-beta.1 (used for pre-release detection)
28	  # -------------------------------------------------
29	  setup:
30	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
31	    outputs:
32	      version: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.version }}
33	      full_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.full_tag }}
34	      raw_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.raw_tag }}
35	      prerelease: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.prerelease }}
36	    steps:
37	      - name: Resolve version strings
38	        id: resolve
39	        run: |
40	          RAW="${{ github.event.inputs.tag != '' && github.event.inputs.tag || github.ref_name }}"
41	          FULL_TAG="${RAW#v}"
42	          VERSION=$(echo "$FULL_TAG" | sed 's/-.*//')
43	          if [[ "$FULL_TAG" == *"-alpha."* || "$FULL_TAG" == *"-beta."* || "$FULL_TAG" == *"-rc."* ]]; then
44	            PRERELEASE=true
45	          else
46	            PRERELEASE=false
47	          fi
48	          echo "raw_tag=${RAW}"         >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
49	          echo "full_tag=${FULL_TAG}"   >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
50	          echo "version=${VERSION}"     >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
51	          echo "prerelease=${PRERELEASE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
52	          echo "RAW_TAG=${RAW}  FULL_TAG=${FULL_TAG}  VERSION=${VERSION}  PRERELEASE=${PRERELEASE}"
53	
54	  # -------------------------------------------------
55	  # Windows - Launch4j wraps JAR into a GUI .exe
56	  # headerType=gui compiles as /SUBSYSTEM:WINDOWS so
57	  # no console window appears on launch.
58	  # Requires Java 11+ installed on the end user's machine.
59	  # -------------------------------------------------
60	  build-windows:
61	    needs: setup
62	    runs-on: windows-latest
63	    env:
64	      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
65	    steps:
66	      - name: Checkout code
67	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
68	        with:
69	          fetch-depth: 0
70	
71	      - name: Set up JDK 17
72	        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
73	        with:
74	          java-version: '17'
75	          distribution: 'temurin'
76	          cache: maven
77	
78	      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version to install so the artifact version in the
79	      # local repo matches the version used by the subsequent package step.
80	      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
81	        shell: pwsh
82	        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests "-Dproject.version=$env:VERSION"
83	
84	      - name: Build with Maven
85	        shell: pwsh
86	        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests "-Dproject.version=$env:VERSION" -pl SwissKit -am
87	
88	      - name: Assemble and compress Windows package
89	        shell: pwsh
90	        run: |
91	          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path release\SwissKit | Out-Null
92	          # FIX: launch4j outfile is target/SwissKit.exe (hardcoded in pom); reference that exact name.
93	          Move-Item SwissKit\target\SwissKit.exe release\SwissKit\SwissKit.exe
94	          Copy-Item README.md release\SwissKit\
95	          Compress-Archive -Path release\SwissKit -DestinationPath "release\SwissKit-${env:VERSION}-windows.zip" -Force
96	          Write-Host "Created: release/SwissKit-${env:VERSION}-windows.zip"
97	
98	      - name: Upload Windows artifact
99	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
100	        with:
101	          name: swisskit-windows
102	          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-windows.zip
103	          retention-days: 30
104	
105	  # -------------------------------------------------
106	  # Linux - plain JAR + shell launcher script
107	  # No bundled JRE; requires Java 11+ on the target machine.
108	  # -------------------------------------------------
109	  build-linux:
110	    needs: setup
111	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
112	    env:
113	      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
114	    steps:
115	      - name: Checkout code
116	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
117	        with:
118	          fetch-depth: 0
119	
120	      - name: Set up JDK 11
121	        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
122	        with:
123	          java-version: '11'
124	          distribution: 'temurin'
125	          cache: maven
126	
127	      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version so install and package use the same version.
128	      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
129	        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION
130	
131	      - name: Build with Maven
132	        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION -pl SwissKit -am
133	
134	      - name: Assemble Linux package
135	        run: |
136	          JAR_FILE=$(find SwissKit/target -maxdepth 1 -name "SwissKit-*.jar" -type f | head -1)
137	          if [ -z "$JAR_FILE" ]; then
138	            echo "ERROR: No SwissKit-*.jar found in SwissKit/target/" >&2
139	            exit 1
140	          fi
141	          mkdir -p release/SwissKit
142	          cp "$JAR_FILE" release/SwissKit/SwissKit.jar
143	          cp README.md   release/SwissKit/
144	          cp SwissKit/src/main/resources/icons/icon-256.png release/SwissKit/swisskit.png
145	
146	      # install.sh self-detects its own location at runtime and writes
147	      # swisskit.desktop with absolute Exec/Icon paths, then registers it
148	      # under ~/.local/share/applications/.  Users run it once after extracting.
149	      - name: Create Linux install script
150	        run: |
151	          printf '%s\n' \
152	            '#!/bin/bash' \
153	            'INSTALL_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"' \
154	            'cat > "$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.desktop" << DESKTOP' \
155	            '[Desktop Entry]' \
156	            'Name=SwissKit' \
157	            'Comment=Swiss Army Knife of Productivity Tools' \
158	            'Exec=$INSTALL_DIR/run.sh' \
159	            'Icon=$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.png' \
160	            'Terminal=false' \
161	            'Type=Application' \
162	            'Categories=Office;Utility;' \
163	            'DESKTOP' \
164	            'mkdir -p "$HOME/.local/share/applications"' \
165	            'cp "$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.desktop" "$HOME/.local/share/applications/"' \
166	            'command -v update-desktop-database &>/dev/null && update-desktop-database "$HOME/.local/share/applications/" 2>/dev/null || true' \
167	            'echo "SwissKit installed. You can now find it in your application launcher."' \
168	            > release/SwissKit/install.sh
169	          chmod +x release/SwissKit/install.sh
170	
171	      # 'exec' replaces the shell process with java for a cleaner process tree.
172	      - name: Write Linux launcher script
173	        run: |
174	          printf '%s\n' \
175	            '#!/bin/bash' \
176	            'SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"' \
177	            'cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"' \
178	            'exec java -jar SwissKit.jar "$@"' \
179	            > release/SwissKit/run.sh
180	          chmod +x release/SwissKit/run.sh
181	
182	      - name: Compress Linux package
183	        run: |
184	          cd release
185	          zip -r "SwissKit-${VERSION}-linux.zip" SwissKit
186	          echo "Created: release/SwissKit-${VERSION}-linux.zip"
187	
188	      - name: Upload Linux artifact
189	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
190	        with:
191	          name: swisskit-linux
192	          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-linux.zip
193	          retention-days: 30
194	
195	  # -------------------------------------------------
196	  # macOS Apple Silicon - hand-crafted .app bundle
197	  #
198	  # Why not jpackage?
199	  #   jpackage app-image always embeds the build-time JRE (~200 MB)
200	  #   even without --runtime-image. A hand-crafted bundle is lightweight
201	  #   and truly JRE-free.
202	  #
203	  # Bundle layout:
204	  #   SwissKit.app/
205	  #     Contents/
206	  #       Info.plist        - identifies this as a GUI app to macOS
207	  #       MacOS/
208	  #         SwissKit        - executable shell script (entry point)
209	  #       Resources/
210	  #         SwissKit.jar    - application JAR
211	  #
212	  # Java resolution order in launcher:
213	  #   1. $JAVA_HOME/bin/java
214	  #   2. java found on PATH
215	  #   3. Newest JVM under /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/
216	  #   If none found, shows a native alert dialog then exits.
217	  # -------------------------------------------------
218	  build-macos:
219	    needs: setup
220	    # FIX: macos-14 is the first runner image with Apple Silicon (M1).
221	    # macos-latest resolves to macos-13 (x86_64) which contradicts the
222	    # 'apple-silicon' label in the artifact name.
223	    runs-on: macos-14
224	    env:
225	      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
226	    steps:
227	      - name: Checkout code
228	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
229	        with:
230	          fetch-depth: 0
231	
232	      - name: Set up JDK 11
233	        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
234	        with:
235	          java-version: '11'
236	          distribution: 'temurin'
237	          cache: maven
238	
239	      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version so install and package use the same version.
240	      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
241	        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION
242	
243	      - name: Build with Maven
244	        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION -pl SwissKit -am
245	
246	      - name: Create .app bundle skeleton
247	        run: |
248	          JAR_FILE=$(find SwissKit/target -maxdepth 1 -name "SwissKit-*.jar" -type f | head -1)
249	          if [ -z "$JAR_FILE" ]; then
250	            echo "ERROR: No SwissKit-*.jar found in SwissKit/target/" >&2
251	            exit 1
252	          fi
253	          mkdir -p release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS
254	          mkdir -p release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources
255	          cp "$JAR_FILE" release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources/SwissKit.jar
256	          cp SwissKit/src/main/resources/icons/SwissKit.icns release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources/
257	
258	      # Write launcher with printf in a separate step to avoid any
259	      # heredoc / YAML block-mapping conflict.
260	      - name: Write macOS launcher script
261	        run: |
262	          printf '%s\n' \
263	            '#!/bin/bash' \
264	            'SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"' \
265	            'RESOURCES_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/../Resources"' \
266	            '' \
267	            '# Resolve java: prefer JAVA_HOME, then PATH,' \
268	            '# then newest JVM under the standard macOS install location.' \
269	            'JAVA_BIN=""' \
270	            'if [ -n "$JAVA_HOME" ] && [ -x "$JAVA_HOME/bin/java" ]; then' \
271	            '  JAVA_BIN="$JAVA_HOME/bin/java"' \
272	            'elif command -v java &>/dev/null; then' \
273	            '  JAVA_BIN="$(command -v java)"' \
274	            'elif [ -d "/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines" ]; then' \
275	            '  JVM_HOME=$(ls /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/ 2>/dev/null | sort -r | head -1)' \
276	            '  CANDIDATE="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/${JVM_HOME}/Contents/Home/bin/java"' \
277	            '  if [ -x "$CANDIDATE" ]; then' \
278	            '    JAVA_BIN="$CANDIDATE"' \
279	            '  fi' \
280	            'fi' \
281	            '' \
282	            'if [ -z "$JAVA_BIN" ]; then' \
283	            '  osascript -e "display alert \"Java Not Found\" message \"SwissKit requires Java 11 or later.\n\nDownload from https://adoptium.net/\" as critical"' \
284	            '  exit 1' \
285	            'fi' \
286	            '' \
287	            'exec "$JAVA_BIN" -jar "$RESOURCES_DIR/SwissKit.jar" "$@"' \
288	            > release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS/SwissKit
289	          chmod +x release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS/SwissKit
290	
291	      # Write Info.plist line-by-line with printf.
292	      # APP_VERSION is assigned from a GitHub Actions output before the
293	      # shell runs, so ${{ }} and shell variables do not conflict.
294	      - name: Write Info.plist
295	        run: |
296	          APP_VERSION="${VERSION}"
297	          INFO="release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Info.plist"
298	          printf '%s\n' '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>'                                 > "$INFO"
299	          printf '%s\n' '<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"'                  >> "$INFO"
300	          printf '%s\n' '  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">'                   >> "$INFO"
301	          printf '%s\n' '<plist version="1.0">'                                                  >> "$INFO"
302	          printf '%s\n' '<dict>'                                                                  >> "$INFO"
303	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleName</key>'                                              >> "$INFO"
304	          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
305	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>'                                       >> "$INFO"
306	          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
307	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>'                                        >> "$INFO"
308	          printf '%s\n' '  <string>com.muskstark.swisskit</string>'                              >> "$INFO"
309	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>'                                           >> "$INFO"
310	          printf '%s'   '  <string>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
311	          printf '%s\n' "$APP_VERSION"'</string>'                                                 >> "$INFO"
312	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>'                                >> "$INFO"
313	          printf '%s'   '  <string>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
314	          printf '%s\n' "$APP_VERSION"'</string>'                                                 >> "$INFO"
315	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>'                                       >> "$INFO"
316	          printf '%s\n' '  <string>APPL</string>'                                                >> "$INFO"
317	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>'                                        >> "$INFO"
318	          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
319	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>'                                    >> "$INFO"
320	          printf '%s\n' '  <string>10.13</string>'                                               >> "$INFO"
321	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>'                                   >> "$INFO"
322	          printf '%s\n' '  <true/>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
323	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>LSUIElement</key>'                                               >> "$INFO"
324	          printf '%s\n' '  <false/>'                                                             >> "$INFO"
325	          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>'                                          >> "$INFO"
326	          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit.icns</string>'                                       >> "$INFO"
327	          printf '%s\n' '</dict>'                                                                 >> "$INFO"
328	          printf '%s\n' '</plist>'                                                                >> "$INFO"
329	          echo "--- Info.plist ---"
330	          cat "$INFO"
331	
332	      - name: Verify and compress .app bundle
333	        run: |
334	          echo "--- Bundle structure ---"
335	          find release/SwissKit.app -type f | sort
336	          cd release
337	          # -y preserves symbolic links inside .app (required for a valid bundle)
338	          zip -ry "SwissKit-${VERSION}-macos-apple-silicon.zip" SwissKit.app
339	          echo "Created: release/SwissKit-${VERSION}-macos-apple-silicon.zip"
340	
341	      - name: Upload macOS artifact
342	        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
343	        with:
344	          name: swisskit-macos
345	          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-macos-apple-silicon.zip
346	          retention-days: 30
347	
348	  # -------------------------------------------------
349	  # Create GitHub Release
350	  # Runs only after all three platform builds succeed.
351	  # -------------------------------------------------
352	  create-release:
353	    needs: [ setup, build-windows, build-linux, build-macos ]
354	    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
355	    env:
356	      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
357	      FULL_TAG: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.full_tag }}
358	      RAW_TAG: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.raw_tag }}
359	      PRERELEASE: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.prerelease }}
360	    steps:
361	      - name: Checkout code
362	        uses: actions/checkout@v4
363	
364	      - name: Download all build artifacts
365	        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
366	        with:
367	          path: artifacts
368	          merge-multiple: true
369	
370	      - name: List downloaded artifacts
371	        run: ls -lh artifacts/
372	
373	      # FIX: pre-release label and warning are derived from FULL_TAG (which
374	      # retains the -beta.1 / -rc.1 suffix), not from VERSION (digits only).
375	      - name: Determine pre-release label and warning
376	        id: prerelease
377	        run: |
378	          if [[ "$PRERELEASE" == "true" ]]; then
379	            echo "label= (Pre-release)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
380	            echo "warning=> ⚠️ This is a pre-release version. Use with caution and please report any bugs!" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
381	          else
382	            echo "label="    >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
383	            echo "warning="  >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
384	          fi
385	
386	      - name: Generate SHA-256 checksums
387	        run: |
388	          cd artifacts
389	          sha256sum *.zip > checksums.txt
390	          cat checksums.txt
391	
392	      - name: Create GitHub Release
393	        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
394	        with:
395	          tag_name: ${{ env.RAW_TAG }}
396	          name: SwissKit ${{ env.FULL_TAG }}${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.label }}
397	          body: |
398	            ## SwissKit ${{ env.FULL_TAG }}${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.label }}
399	
400	            ${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.warning }}
401	
402	            ### Requirements
403	            Java 11 or later must be installed on your system.
404	
405	            | Platform | Install |
406	            |---|---|
407	            | Windows | [Download from Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/) |
408	            | macOS | `brew install --cask temurin@11` |
409	            | Linux (Debian/Ubuntu) | `sudo apt install openjdk-11-jre` |
410	            | Linux (Fedora/RHEL) | `sudo dnf install java-11-openjdk` |
411	
412	            ### Downloads
413	
414	            | Platform | File | How to run |
415	            |---|---|---|
416	            | Windows 10/11 (64-bit) | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-windows.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.exe` |
417	            | Linux x64 | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-linux.zip` | Extract then run `./run.sh` |
418	            | macOS Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-macos-apple-silicon.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.app` |
419	
420	            ### What's New
421	            See the [CHANGELOG](https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/#/changelog) for details.
422	
423	            ---
424	            SHA-256 checksums are provided in `checksums.txt`.
425	            This release was automatically created by GitHub Actions.
426	          files: |
427	            artifacts/*.zip
428	            artifacts/checksums.txt
429	          draft: false
430	          prerelease: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.prerelease == 'true' }}
431	          generate_release_notes: true
432	        env:
433	          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
