file.content:
name: Release

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: 'Tag to release (e.g. v1.2.3 or v1.0.0-beta.1)'
        required: true
        type: string
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+'
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-alpha\.[0-9]+'
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-beta\.[0-9]+'
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-rc\.[0-9]+'

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Resolve version strings once; all build jobs use
  # these outputs so there is a single source of truth.
  #
  # RAW_TAG  : original tag, e.g. v2.1.0-beta.1
  # VERSION  : semver digits only, e.g. 2.1.0   (used as Maven project.version)
  # FULL_TAG : tag without leading 'v', e.g. 2.1.0-beta.1 (used for pre-release detection)
  # -------------------------------------------------
  setup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.version }}
      full_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.full_tag }}
      raw_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.raw_tag }}
      prerelease: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.prerelease }}
    steps:
      - name: Resolve version strings
        id: resolve
        run: |
          RAW="${{ github.event.inputs.tag != '' && github.event.inputs.tag || github.ref_name }}"
          FULL_TAG="${RAW#v}"
          VERSION=$(echo "$FULL_TAG" | sed 's/-.*//')
          if [[ "$FULL_TAG" == *"-alpha."* || "$FULL_TAG" == *"-beta."* || "$FULL_TAG" == *"-rc."* ]]; then
            PRERELEASE=true
          else
            PRERELEASE=false
          fi
          echo "raw_tag=${RAW}"         >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "full_tag=${FULL_TAG}"   >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=${VERSION}"     >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "prerelease=${PRERELEASE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "RAW_TAG=${RAW}  FULL_TAG=${FULL_TAG}  VERSION=${VERSION}  PRERELEASE=${PRERELEASE}"

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Windows - Launch4j wraps JAR into a GUI .exe
  # headerType=gui compiles as /SUBSYSTEM:WINDOWS so
  # no console window appears on launch.
  # Requires Java 11+ installed on the end user's machine.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  build-windows:
    needs: setup
    runs-on: windows-latest
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'
          cache: maven

      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version to install so the artifact version in the
      # local repo matches the version used by the subsequent package step.
      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
        shell: pwsh
        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests "-Dproject.version=$env:VERSION"

      - name: Build with Maven
        shell: pwsh
        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests "-Dproject.version=$env:VERSION" -pl SwissKit -am

      - name: Assemble and compress Windows package
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path release\SwissKit | Out-Null
          # FIX: launch4j outfile is target/SwissKit.exe (hardcoded in pom); reference that exact name.
          Move-Item SwissKit\target\SwissKit.exe release\SwissKit\SwissKit.exe
          Copy-Item README.md release\SwissKit\
          Compress-Archive -Path release\SwissKit -DestinationPath "release\SwissKit-${env:VERSION}-windows.zip" -Force
          Write-Host "Created: release/SwissKit-${env:VERSION}-windows.zip"

      - name: Upload Windows artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: swisskit-windows
          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-windows.zip
          retention-days: 30

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Linux - plain JAR + shell launcher script
  # No bundled JRE; requires Java 11+ on the target machine.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  build-linux:
    needs: setup
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
          cache: maven

      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version so install and package use the same version.
      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION

      - name: Build with Maven
        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION -pl SwissKit -am

      - name: Assemble Linux package
        run: |
          JAR_FILE=$(find SwissKit/target -maxdepth 1 -name "SwissKit-*.jar" -type f | head -1)
          if [ -z "$JAR_FILE" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: No SwissKit-*.jar found in SwissKit/target/" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          mkdir -p release/SwissKit
          cp "$JAR_FILE" release/SwissKit/SwissKit.jar
          cp README.md   release/SwissKit/
          cp SwissKit/src/main/resources/icons/icon-256.png release/SwissKit/swisskit.png

      # install.sh self-detects its own location at runtime and writes
      # swisskit.desktop with absolute Exec/Icon paths, then registers it
      # under ~/.local/share/applications/.  Users run it once after extracting.
      - name: Create Linux install script
        run: |
          printf '%s\n' \
            '#!/bin/bash' \
            'INSTALL_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"' \
            'cat > "$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.desktop" << DESKTOP' \
            '[Desktop Entry]' \
            'Name=SwissKit' \
            'Comment=Swiss Army Knife of Productivity Tools' \
            'Exec=$INSTALL_DIR/run.sh' \
            'Icon=$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.png' \
            'Terminal=false' \
            'Type=Application' \
            'Categories=Office;Utility;' \
            'DESKTOP' \
            'mkdir -p "$HOME/.local/share/applications"' \
            'cp "$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.desktop" "$HOME/.local/share/applications/"' \
            'command -v update-desktop-database &>/dev/null && update-desktop-database "$HOME/.local/share/applications/" 2>/dev/null || true' \
            'echo "SwissKit installed. You can now find it in your application launcher."' \
            > release/SwissKit/install.sh
          chmod +x release/SwissKit/install.sh

      # 'exec' replaces the shell process with java for a cleaner process tree.
      - name: Write Linux launcher script
        run: |
          printf '%s\n' \
            '#!/bin/bash' \
            'SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"' \
            'cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"' \
            'exec java -jar SwissKit.jar "$@"' \
            > release/SwissKit/run.sh
          chmod +x release/SwissKit/run.sh

      - name: Compress Linux package
        run: |
          cd release
          zip -r "SwissKit-${VERSION}-linux.zip" SwissKit
          echo "Created: release/SwissKit-${VERSION}-linux.zip"

      - name: Upload Linux artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: swisskit-linux
          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-linux.zip
          retention-days: 30

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # macOS Apple Silicon - hand-crafted .app bundle
  #
  # Why not jpackage?
  #   jpackage app-image always embeds the build-time JRE (~200 MB)
  #   even without --runtime-image. A hand-crafted bundle is lightweight
  #   and truly JRE-free.
  #
  # Bundle layout:
  #   SwissKit.app/
  #     Contents/
  #       Info.plist        - identifies this as a GUI app to macOS
  #       MacOS/
  #         SwissKit        - executable shell script (entry point)
  #       Resources/
  #         SwissKit.jar    - application JAR
  #
  # Java resolution order in launcher:
  #   1. $JAVA_HOME/bin/java
  #   2. java found on PATH
  #   3. Newest JVM under /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/
  #   If none found, shows a native alert dialog then exits.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  build-macos:
    needs: setup
    # FIX: macos-14 is the first runner image with Apple Silicon (M1).
    # macos-latest resolves to macos-13 (x86_64) which contradicts the
    # 'apple-silicon' label in the artifact name.
    runs-on: macos-14
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
          cache: maven

      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version so install and package use the same version.
      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION

      - name: Build with Maven
        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION -pl SwissKit -am

      - name: Create .app bundle skeleton
        run: |
          JAR_FILE=$(find SwissKit/target -maxdepth 1 -name "SwissKit-*.jar" -type f | head -1)
          if [ -z "$JAR_FILE" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: No SwissKit-*.jar found in SwissKit/target/" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          mkdir -p release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS
          mkdir -p release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources
          cp "$JAR_FILE" release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources/SwissKit.jar
          cp SwissKit/src/main/resources/icons/SwissKit.icns release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources/

      # Write launcher with printf in a separate step to avoid any
      # heredoc / YAML block-mapping conflict.
      - name: Write macOS launcher script
        run: |
          printf '%s\n' \
            '#!/bin/bash' \
            'SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"' \
            'RESOURCES_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/../Resources"' \
            '' \
            '# Resolve java: prefer JAVA_HOME, then PATH,' \
            '# then newest JVM under the standard macOS install location.' \
            'JAVA_BIN=""' \
            'if [ -n "$JAVA_HOME" ] && [ -x "$JAVA_HOME/bin/java" ]; then' \
            '  JAVA_BIN="$JAVA_HOME/bin/java"' \
            'elif command -v java &>/dev/null; then' \
            '  JAVA_BIN="$(command -v java)"' \
            'elif [ -d "/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines" ]; then' \
            '  JVM_HOME=$(ls /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/ 2>/dev/null | sort -r | head -1)' \
            '  CANDIDATE="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/${JVM_HOME}/Contents/Home/bin/java"' \
            '  if [ -x "$CANDIDATE" ]; then' \
            '    JAVA_BIN="$CANDIDATE"' \
            '  fi' \
            'fi' \
            '' \
            'if [ -z "$JAVA_BIN" ]; then' \
            '  osascript -e "display alert \"Java Not Found\" message \"SwissKit requires Java 11 or later.\n\nDownload from https://adoptium.net/\" as critical"' \
            '  exit 1' \
            'fi' \
            '' \
            'exec "$JAVA_BIN" -jar "$RESOURCES_DIR/SwissKit.jar" "$@"' \
            > release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS/SwissKit
          chmod +x release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS/SwissKit

      # Write Info.plist line-by-line with printf.
      # APP_VERSION is assigned from a GitHub Actions output before the
      # shell runs, so ${{ }} and shell variables do not conflict.
      - name: Write Info.plist
        run: |
          APP_VERSION="${VERSION}"
          INFO="release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Info.plist"
          printf '%s\n' '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>'                                 > "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"'                  >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">'                   >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '<plist version="1.0">'                                                  >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '<dict>'                                                                  >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleName</key>'                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>'                                       >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>'                                        >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>com.muskstark.swisskit</string>'                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>'                                           >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s'   '  <string>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' "$APP_VERSION"'</string>'                                                 >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>'                                >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s'   '  <string>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' "$APP_VERSION"'</string>'                                                 >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>'                                       >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>APPL</string>'                                                >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>'                                        >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>'                                    >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>10.13</string>'                                               >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>'                                   >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <true/>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>LSUIElement</key>'                                               >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <false/>'                                                             >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>'                                          >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit.icns</string>'                                       >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '</dict>'                                                                 >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '</plist>'                                                                >> "$INFO"
          echo "--- Info.plist ---"
          cat "$INFO"

      - name: Verify and compress .app bundle
        run: |
          echo "--- Bundle structure ---"
          find release/SwissKit.app -type f | sort
          cd release
          # -y preserves symbolic links inside .app (required for a valid bundle)
          zip -ry "SwissKit-${VERSION}-macos-apple-silicon.zip" SwissKit.app
          echo "Created: release/SwissKit-${VERSION}-macos-apple-silicon.zip"

      - name: Upload macOS artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: swisskit-macos
          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-macos-apple-silicon.zip
          retention-days: 30

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Create GitHub Release
  # Runs only after all three platform builds succeed.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  create-release:
    needs: [ setup, build-windows, build-linux, build-macos ]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
      FULL_TAG: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.full_tag }}
      RAW_TAG: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.raw_tag }}
      PRERELEASE: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.prerelease }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download all build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: List downloaded artifacts
        run: ls -lh artifacts/

      # FIX: pre-release label and warning are derived from FULL_TAG (which
      # retains the -beta.1 / -rc.1 suffix), not from VERSION (digits only).
      - name: Determine pre-release label and warning
        id: prerelease
        run: |
          if [[ "$PRERELEASE" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "label= (Pre-release)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "warning=> ⚠️ This is a pre-release version. Use with caution and please report any bugs!" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "label="    >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "warning="  >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Generate SHA-256 checksums
        run: |
          cd artifacts
          sha256sum *.zip > checksums.txt
          cat checksums.txt

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.RAW_TAG }}
          name: SwissKit ${{ env.FULL_TAG }}${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.label }}
          body: |
            ## SwissKit ${{ env.FULL_TAG }}${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.label }}

            ${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.warning }}

            ### Requirements
            Java 11 or later must be installed on your system.

            | Platform | Install |
            |---|---|
            | Windows | [Download from Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/) |
            | macOS | `brew install --cask temurin@11` |
            | Linux (Debian/Ubuntu) | `sudo apt install openjdk-11-jre` |
            | Linux (Fedora/RHEL) | `sudo dnf install java-11-openjdk` |

            ### Downloads

            | Platform | File | How to run |
            |---|---|---|
            | Windows 10/11 (64-bit) | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-windows.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.exe` |
            | Linux x64 | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-linux.zip` | Extract then run `./run.sh` |
            | macOS Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-macos-apple-silicon.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.app` |

            ### What's New
            See the [CHANGELOG](https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/#/changelog) for details.

            ---
            SHA-256 checksums are provided in `checksums.txt`.
            This release was automatically created by GitHub Actions.
          files: |
            artifacts/*.zip
            artifacts/checksums.txt
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.prerelease == 'true' }}
          generate_release_notes: true
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

The user wants to test the plugin store functionality locally. The issue is that the OnlineStorePane fetches from a remote URL (`https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/plugins/store.json`), which requires an internet connection and the file to be deployed to GitHub Pages first.

To test locally, they need a local HTTP server serving the `docs/plugins/store.json` file, and need to point the settings to `http://localhost:<port>/plugins/store.json`.

最简单的办法是用 Python 本地起一个 HTTP 服务，然后修改商店地址指向本地。

**Step 1**: 在项目根目录启动本地 HTTP 服务（`docs/` 目录下）：

```bash
cd /Users/phoebej/Develop/Java/SwissKitJ/docs
python3 -m http.server 8888
```

**Step 2**: 在应用的 Settings → Plugin Store 中将 URL 改为：

```
http://localhost:8888/plugins/store.json
```

然后切回 Plugin Store 点 Refresh 就能看到插件列表了。

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user performed a series of UI refactoring tasks on a JavaFX application called SwissKitJ:
   - Redesigned the settings UI to match the main app's glassmorphism (液态玻璃) style
   - Converted the settings from TabPane navigation to sidebar menu layout
   - Consolidated all backup folders to project root `backup/` directory
   - Moved plugin store URL configuration into Settings (under SETTINGS section, not BUILD-IN TOOLS)
   - Removed Plugin tab entirely from settings (plugin management moved elsewhere)
   - Moved Email into BUILD-IN TOOLS sub-section of settings sidebar
   - Created `docs/plugins/store.json` for the plugin store endpoint
   - Set up local testing mechanism for the plugin store via system property override
   - Fixed label text wrapping issue in OnlineStorePane

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - JavaFX CSS class-based styling (glass.css with classes like `.glass-dialog`, `.glass-field`, `.glass-btn-primary`, etc.)
   - Sidebar + StackPane visibility-based navigation pattern (NavItem active states, page switching)
   - H2 database with MyBatis for persisting settings (AppSettingMapper, AppSettingEntity)
   - Maven multi-module build: `SwissKitJ-Api` must be installed before `SwissKit`
   - GitHub Pages via docsify (Runner Image Provisioner) — `docs/` served at `muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/`
   - Plugin store JSON format: `[{id, name, description, version, jarUrl, iconStyle, category}]`
   - System property override for local store testing: `-Dstore.url=http://localhost:8888/plugins/store.json`
   - NavItem re-use from `Sidebar.NavItem` for all sidebar menus

3. Files and Code Sections:
   - **`SwissKit/src/main/resources/css/glass.css`**
     - Added 8 new CSS class blocks for glassmorphism consistency across all UI components
     - Classes added: `.glass-dialog`, `.glass-field` (focused state), `.glass-field-label`, `.section-header`, `.glass-combo`, `.glass-table` (with column headers, selected/hover rows), `.glass-checkbox` (with :selected pseudo), `.glass-btn-primary/secondary` (with :hover/:pressed)
     - Also added `.glass-tab-pane` for TabPane styling (later removed when converted to sidebar)

   - **`SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/ui/setting/SwissKitJSettingUi.java`**
     - Complete sidebar layout replacing TabPane — key structure:
     ```java
     Node generalPage = buildGeneralTab();
     Node storePage = buildPluginStoreSettings();  // NEW
     Node emailPage = buildEmailTab();
     
     // Sidebar with SETTINGS (General, Plugin Store) and BUILD-IN TOOLS (Email)
     NavItem generalNav = sidebarNavItem("⚙", "General");
     NavItem storeNav = sidebarNavItem("🏪", "Plugin Store");
     NavItem emailNav = sidebarNavItem("✉", "Email");
     ```
     - `getStoreUrl()` with system property override:
     ```java
     public static String getStoreUrl() {
         String override = System.getProperty("store.url");
         if (override != null && !override.isBlank()) {
             return override;
         }
         // ... DB lookup
     }
     ```
     - Removed: `buildPluginTab()`, `deployPlugin()`, `refreshPluginList()`, `PluginJarInfo` inner class, `pluginPathField`/`pluginTable` static fields
     - Removed unused imports: `FileChooser`, `Files`, `Path`, `StandardCopyOption`, `PluginManagerMapper`, `PluginManagerEntity`
     - Store URL key: `"plugin.store.url"`, default: `"https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/plugins/store.json"`

   - **`SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/ui/store/OnlineStorePane.java`**
     - Removed `DEFAULT_STORE_URL` constant, `urlField` TextField, `currentStoreUrl` field
     - URL now reads from `SwissKitJSettingUi.getStoreUrl()`
     - Removed `fieldStyle()` helper method (now uses glass.css classes)
     - Desc label wrapping fix:
     ```java
     desc.maxWidthProperty().bind(
         widthProperty().subtract(48)  // 24px padding on each side
     );
     ```
     - Changed description text to: `"Browse and install plugins from the online store."`
     - Removed `TextField` import (no longer used)

   - **`SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/ui/store/PluginStoreUi.java`**
     - Converted from TabPane to sidebar layout (same pattern as settings)
     - Sidebar with "STORE" section: Online Store (🌐) and Local Install (📦)
     - Uses `Sidebar.NavItem` with visibility-based page switching

   - **`SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/ui/content/ContentArea.java`**
     - Added `sp.setFitToHeight(true)` to `buildPageScrollPane()` so settings fills ContentArea height

   - **`SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/ui/MainWindow.java`**
     - Removed StackPane wrapper around settings page — now passes Node directly
     - Added `import javafx.scene.Node;`

   - **`docs/plugins/store.json`** (NEW FILE)
     - 7 built-in plugins with full metadata (id, name, description, version, jarUrl, iconStyle, category)
     - URL pattern: `https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/plugins/builtin.{id}.jar`

   - **`CLAUDE.md`** — Updated backup paths from `SwissKit/backup/` to `backup/SwissKit/`

4. Errors and fixes:
   - **Build errors (57 compilation failures)**: `SwissKitJ-Api` not installed first → Fixed by running `mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests` before building SwissKit module
   - **`fieldStyle()` method still present after conversion**: Removed duplicate by editing too much — fixed by restoring `FIELD_STYLE_CLASS` constant
   - **Duplicate Tab/TabPane imports**: Removed duplicate `import` statements for Tab and TabPane (lines 22-23 and 29-30) since TabPane no longer used
   - **`wrappingWidthProperty()` not available on Label**: Changed to `maxWidthProperty().bind(widthProperty().subtract(48))`
   - **Maven circular dependency on `swisskit-summer.github.io` repo**: Repo didn't exist. User selected "Use MuskStark account". Created `docs/plugins/store.json` at correct path for `muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/plugins/store.json`

5. Problem Solving:
   - Settings and PluginStore UIs now use identical sidebar layout patterns — shared `NavItem` class from `Sidebar`, shared CSS classes from `glass.css`
   - Local plugin store testing solved via Java system property `-Dstore.url=` — no database editing or hosts file modification needed
   - Settings page filling ContentArea solved by adding `fitToHeight=true` on the page ScrollPane and removing the StackPane wrapper

6. All user messages:
   - `/init` — triggered CLAUDE.md creation (already done, no-op response)
   - "将该界面的UI修改成和主程序UI风格一直的液态玻璃状态" — Redesign settings UI with glassmorphism
   - "tabe页看不清楚，标签使用圆角，选中是页现实圆角" — Fix tab styling with rounded corners and selected state
   - "setting界面没有填充满ContentArea" — Fix settings filling ContentArea
   - "将setting界面从tabe改成侧边菜单栏" — Convert settings from TabPane to sidebar menu
   - "settingUI要和pluginstoreui相同，不需要外框线" — Remove outer border from settings
   - "setting中移除plugin相关配置及界面，同时将侧边栏email作为二级菜单移入BuildInToolSettings菜单中" — Remove plugin tab and nest Email under BUILD-IN TOOLS
   - "将所有的backup文件夹归档到项目根文件" — Move all backup folders to project root `backup/`
   - "将插件商店的地址配置移到设置界面中，放入到settings一级菜单下" — Add Plugin Store URL config to settings top-level
   - "修改pluginstore onlinestore界面，不再需要在该界面设置商店地址" — Remove URL field from OnlineStorePane
   - (Build output pasted — user showing Maven build results)
   - "在界面上显示不全Label desc = new Label(...)" — Label text getting cut off
   - "在界面上显示不全Label desc = new Label(...) 还是只显示到the store URL" — Wrapping still broken after first fix
   - "使用gh完成https://swisskit-summer.github.io/plugins/store.json的创建，并将官方插件编辑进去" — Create store.json for plugin store
   - "使用docsify实现" — Docs uses docsify, docs/ served as GitHub Pages
   - "通过docs实现store.json.docs下的文件会被编译成githubpage。地址是muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/" — Pages URL confirmed
   - "怎么实现本地测试插件商店是否功能正确" — Ask how to test plugin store locally
   - "是否可以在本地劫持商店地址" — Ask if we can hijack store URL locally
   - "commit" — (Summary request)

7. Pending Tasks:
   - No explicit pending tasks. All requested changes have been implemented.

8. Current Work:
   The most recent work was adding a local testing mechanism for the plugin store URL. The final edit added a system property override at the start of `getStoreUrl()`:
   ```java
   public static String getStoreUrl() {
       String override = System.getProperty("store.url");
       if (override != null && !override.isBlank()) {
           return override;
       }
       try (SqlSession session = DatabaseInit.getSqlSession()) {
           // ... DB lookup
       }
   }
   ```
   This allows running the app with `-Dstore.url=http://localhost:8888/plugins/store.json` to test a local store.json without modifying the database.

   The `docs/plugins/store.json` has been created with 7 built-in plugins and the default URL updated to `https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/plugins/store.json`.

9. Optional Next Step:
   The last task was adding the local store testing mechanism. No further explicit requests were made. The natural next step would be to rebuild the project to verify the latest `getStoreUrl()` change compiles cleanly, since the user has been iteratively testing via Maven builds. However, the user has not explicitly requested this.

file.content:
name: Release

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      tag:
        description: 'Tag to release (e.g. v1.2.3 or v1.0.0-beta.1)'
        required: true
        type: string
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+'
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-alpha\.[0-9]+'
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-beta\.[0-9]+'
      - 'v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+-rc\.[0-9]+'

permissions:
  contents: write

jobs:
  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Resolve version strings once; all build jobs use
  # these outputs so there is a single source of truth.
  #
  # RAW_TAG  : original tag, e.g. v2.1.0-beta.1
  # VERSION  : semver digits only, e.g. 2.1.0   (used as Maven project.version)
  # FULL_TAG : tag without leading 'v', e.g. 2.1.0-beta.1 (used for pre-release detection)
  # -------------------------------------------------
  setup:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.version }}
      full_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.full_tag }}
      raw_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.raw_tag }}
      prerelease: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.prerelease }}
    steps:
      - name: Resolve version strings
        id: resolve
        run: |
          RAW="${{ github.event.inputs.tag != '' && github.event.inputs.tag || github.ref_name }}"
          FULL_TAG="${RAW#v}"
          VERSION=$(echo "$FULL_TAG" | sed 's/-.*//')
          if [[ "$FULL_TAG" == *"-alpha."* || "$FULL_TAG" == *"-beta."* || "$FULL_TAG" == *"-rc."* ]]; then
            PRERELEASE=true
          else
            PRERELEASE=false
          fi
          echo "raw_tag=${RAW}"         >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "full_tag=${FULL_TAG}"   >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version=${VERSION}"     >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "prerelease=${PRERELEASE}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "RAW_TAG=${RAW}  FULL_TAG=${FULL_TAG}  VERSION=${VERSION}  PRERELEASE=${PRERELEASE}"

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Windows - Launch4j wraps JAR into a GUI .exe
  # headerType=gui compiles as /SUBSYSTEM:WINDOWS so
  # no console window appears on launch.
  # Requires Java 11+ installed on the end user's machine.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  build-windows:
    needs: setup
    runs-on: windows-latest
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 17
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '17'
          distribution: 'temurin'
          cache: maven

      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version to install so the artifact version in the
      # local repo matches the version used by the subsequent package step.
      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
        shell: pwsh
        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests "-Dproject.version=$env:VERSION"

      - name: Build with Maven
        shell: pwsh
        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests "-Dproject.version=$env:VERSION" -pl SwissKit -am

      - name: Assemble and compress Windows package
        shell: pwsh
        run: |
          New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force -Path release\SwissKit | Out-Null
          # FIX: launch4j outfile is target/SwissKit.exe (hardcoded in pom); reference that exact name.
          Move-Item SwissKit\target\SwissKit.exe release\SwissKit\SwissKit.exe
          Copy-Item README.md release\SwissKit\
          Compress-Archive -Path release\SwissKit -DestinationPath "release\SwissKit-${env:VERSION}-windows.zip" -Force
          Write-Host "Created: release/SwissKit-${env:VERSION}-windows.zip"

      - name: Upload Windows artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: swisskit-windows
          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-windows.zip
          retention-days: 30

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Linux - plain JAR + shell launcher script
  # No bundled JRE; requires Java 11+ on the target machine.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  build-linux:
    needs: setup
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
          cache: maven

      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version so install and package use the same version.
      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION

      - name: Build with Maven
        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION -pl SwissKit -am

      - name: Assemble Linux package
        run: |
          JAR_FILE=$(find SwissKit/target -maxdepth 1 -name "SwissKit-*.jar" -type f | head -1)
          if [ -z "$JAR_FILE" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: No SwissKit-*.jar found in SwissKit/target/" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          mkdir -p release/SwissKit
          cp "$JAR_FILE" release/SwissKit/SwissKit.jar
          cp README.md   release/SwissKit/
          cp SwissKit/src/main/resources/icons/icon-256.png release/SwissKit/swisskit.png

      # install.sh self-detects its own location at runtime and writes
      # swisskit.desktop with absolute Exec/Icon paths, then registers it
      # under ~/.local/share/applications/.  Users run it once after extracting.
      - name: Create Linux install script
        run: |
          printf '%s\n' \
            '#!/bin/bash' \
            'INSTALL_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"' \
            'cat > "$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.desktop" << DESKTOP' \
            '[Desktop Entry]' \
            'Name=SwissKit' \
            'Comment=Swiss Army Knife of Productivity Tools' \
            'Exec=$INSTALL_DIR/run.sh' \
            'Icon=$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.png' \
            'Terminal=false' \
            'Type=Application' \
            'Categories=Office;Utility;' \
            'DESKTOP' \
            'mkdir -p "$HOME/.local/share/applications"' \
            'cp "$INSTALL_DIR/swisskit.desktop" "$HOME/.local/share/applications/"' \
            'command -v update-desktop-database &>/dev/null && update-desktop-database "$HOME/.local/share/applications/" 2>/dev/null || true' \
            'echo "SwissKit installed. You can now find it in your application launcher."' \
            > release/SwissKit/install.sh
          chmod +x release/SwissKit/install.sh

      # 'exec' replaces the shell process with java for a cleaner process tree.
      - name: Write Linux launcher script
        run: |
          printf '%s\n' \
            '#!/bin/bash' \
            'SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"' \
            'cd "$SCRIPT_DIR"' \
            'exec java -jar SwissKit.jar "$@"' \
            > release/SwissKit/run.sh
          chmod +x release/SwissKit/run.sh

      - name: Compress Linux package
        run: |
          cd release
          zip -r "SwissKit-${VERSION}-linux.zip" SwissKit
          echo "Created: release/SwissKit-${VERSION}-linux.zip"

      - name: Upload Linux artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: swisskit-linux
          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-linux.zip
          retention-days: 30

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # macOS Apple Silicon - hand-crafted .app bundle
  #
  # Why not jpackage?
  #   jpackage app-image always embeds the build-time JRE (~200 MB)
  #   even without --runtime-image. A hand-crafted bundle is lightweight
  #   and truly JRE-free.
  #
  # Bundle layout:
  #   SwissKit.app/
  #     Contents/
  #       Info.plist        - identifies this as a GUI app to macOS
  #       MacOS/
  #         SwissKit        - executable shell script (entry point)
  #       Resources/
  #         SwissKit.jar    - application JAR
  #
  # Java resolution order in launcher:
  #   1. $JAVA_HOME/bin/java
  #   2. java found on PATH
  #   3. Newest JVM under /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/
  #   If none found, shows a native alert dialog then exits.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  build-macos:
    needs: setup
    # FIX: macos-14 is the first runner image with Apple Silicon (M1).
    # macos-latest resolves to macos-13 (x86_64) which contradicts the
    # 'apple-silicon' label in the artifact name.
    runs-on: macos-14
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0

      - name: Set up JDK 11
        uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          java-version: '11'
          distribution: 'temurin'
          cache: maven

      # FIX: pass -Dproject.version so install and package use the same version.
      - name: Install SwissKitJ-Api to local repository
        run: mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION

      - name: Build with Maven
        run: mvn clean package -DskipTests -Dproject.version=$VERSION -pl SwissKit -am

      - name: Create .app bundle skeleton
        run: |
          JAR_FILE=$(find SwissKit/target -maxdepth 1 -name "SwissKit-*.jar" -type f | head -1)
          if [ -z "$JAR_FILE" ]; then
            echo "ERROR: No SwissKit-*.jar found in SwissKit/target/" >&2
            exit 1
          fi
          mkdir -p release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS
          mkdir -p release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources
          cp "$JAR_FILE" release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources/SwissKit.jar
          cp SwissKit/src/main/resources/icons/SwissKit.icns release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Resources/

      # Write launcher with printf in a separate step to avoid any
      # heredoc / YAML block-mapping conflict.
      - name: Write macOS launcher script
        run: |
          printf '%s\n' \
            '#!/bin/bash' \
            'SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "$0")" && pwd)"' \
            'RESOURCES_DIR="$SCRIPT_DIR/../Resources"' \
            '' \
            '# Resolve java: prefer JAVA_HOME, then PATH,' \
            '# then newest JVM under the standard macOS install location.' \
            'JAVA_BIN=""' \
            'if [ -n "$JAVA_HOME" ] && [ -x "$JAVA_HOME/bin/java" ]; then' \
            '  JAVA_BIN="$JAVA_HOME/bin/java"' \
            'elif command -v java &>/dev/null; then' \
            '  JAVA_BIN="$(command -v java)"' \
            'elif [ -d "/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines" ]; then' \
            '  JVM_HOME=$(ls /Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/ 2>/dev/null | sort -r | head -1)' \
            '  CANDIDATE="/Library/Java/JavaVirtualMachines/${JVM_HOME}/Contents/Home/bin/java"' \
            '  if [ -x "$CANDIDATE" ]; then' \
            '    JAVA_BIN="$CANDIDATE"' \
            '  fi' \
            'fi' \
            '' \
            'if [ -z "$JAVA_BIN" ]; then' \
            '  osascript -e "display alert \"Java Not Found\" message \"SwissKit requires Java 11 or later.\n\nDownload from https://adoptium.net/\" as critical"' \
            '  exit 1' \
            'fi' \
            '' \
            'exec "$JAVA_BIN" -jar "$RESOURCES_DIR/SwissKit.jar" "$@"' \
            > release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS/SwissKit
          chmod +x release/SwissKit.app/Contents/MacOS/SwissKit

      # Write Info.plist line-by-line with printf.
      # APP_VERSION is assigned from a GitHub Actions output before the
      # shell runs, so ${{ }} and shell variables do not conflict.
      - name: Write Info.plist
        run: |
          APP_VERSION="${VERSION}"
          INFO="release/SwissKit.app/Contents/Info.plist"
          printf '%s\n' '<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>'                                 > "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '<!DOCTYPE plist PUBLIC "-//Apple//DTD PLIST 1.0//EN"'                  >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  "http://www.apple.com/DTDs/PropertyList-1.0.dtd">'                   >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '<plist version="1.0">'                                                  >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '<dict>'                                                                  >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleName</key>'                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleDisplayName</key>'                                       >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleIdentifier</key>'                                        >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>com.muskstark.swisskit</string>'                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleVersion</key>'                                           >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s'   '  <string>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' "$APP_VERSION"'</string>'                                                 >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleShortVersionString</key>'                                >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s'   '  <string>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' "$APP_VERSION"'</string>'                                                 >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundlePackageType</key>'                                       >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>APPL</string>'                                                >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleExecutable</key>'                                        >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit</string>'                                            >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>LSMinimumSystemVersion</key>'                                    >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>10.13</string>'                                               >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>NSHighResolutionCapable</key>'                                   >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <true/>'                                                              >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>LSUIElement</key>'                                               >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <false/>'                                                             >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <key>CFBundleIconFile</key>'                                          >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '  <string>SwissKit.icns</string>'                                       >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '</dict>'                                                                 >> "$INFO"
          printf '%s\n' '</plist>'                                                                >> "$INFO"
          echo "--- Info.plist ---"
          cat "$INFO"

      - name: Verify and compress .app bundle
        run: |
          echo "--- Bundle structure ---"
          find release/SwissKit.app -type f | sort
          cd release
          # -y preserves symbolic links inside .app (required for a valid bundle)
          zip -ry "SwissKit-${VERSION}-macos-apple-silicon.zip" SwissKit.app
          echo "Created: release/SwissKit-${VERSION}-macos-apple-silicon.zip"

      - name: Upload macOS artifact
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: swisskit-macos
          path: release/SwissKit-${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}-macos-apple-silicon.zip
          retention-days: 30

  # -------------------------------------------------
  # Create GitHub Release
  # Runs only after all three platform builds succeed.
  # -------------------------------------------------
  create-release:
    needs: [ setup, build-windows, build-linux, build-macos ]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    env:
      VERSION: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.version }}
      FULL_TAG: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.full_tag }}
      RAW_TAG: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.raw_tag }}
      PRERELEASE: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.prerelease }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Download all build artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: artifacts
          merge-multiple: true

      - name: List downloaded artifacts
        run: ls -lh artifacts/

      # FIX: pre-release label and warning are derived from FULL_TAG (which
      # retains the -beta.1 / -rc.1 suffix), not from VERSION (digits only).
      - name: Determine pre-release label and warning
        id: prerelease
        run: |
          if [[ "$PRERELEASE" == "true" ]]; then
            echo "label= (Pre-release)" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "warning=> ⚠️ This is a pre-release version. Use with caution and please report any bugs!" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          else
            echo "label="    >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
            echo "warning="  >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          fi

      - name: Generate SHA-256 checksums
        run: |
          cd artifacts
          sha256sum *.zip > checksums.txt
          cat checksums.txt

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v2
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ env.RAW_TAG }}
          name: SwissKit ${{ env.FULL_TAG }}${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.label }}
          body: |
            ## SwissKit ${{ env.FULL_TAG }}${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.label }}

            ${{ steps.prerelease.outputs.warning }}

            ### Requirements
            Java 11 or later must be installed on your system.

            | Platform | Install |
            |---|---|
            | Windows | [Download from Adoptium](https://adoptium.net/) |
            | macOS | `brew install --cask temurin@11` |
            | Linux (Debian/Ubuntu) | `sudo apt install openjdk-11-jre` |
            | Linux (Fedora/RHEL) | `sudo dnf install java-11-openjdk` |

            ### Downloads

            | Platform | File | How to run |
            |---|---|---|
            | Windows 10/11 (64-bit) | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-windows.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.exe` |
            | Linux x64 | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-linux.zip` | Extract then run `./run.sh` |
            | macOS Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) | `SwissKit-${{ env.VERSION }}-macos-apple-silicon.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.app` |

            ### What's New
            See the [CHANGELOG](https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/#/changelog) for details.

            ---
            SHA-256 checksums are provided in `checksums.txt`.
            This release was automatically created by GitHub Actions.
          files: |
            artifacts/*.zip
            artifacts/checksums.txt
          draft: false
          prerelease: ${{ needs.setup.outputs.prerelease == 'true' }}
          generate_release_notes: true
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

[
    {

# Plan: Settings UI Glassmorphism Consistency

## Context

The settings UI (`SwissKitJSettingUi.java`) has inline glass-like styles but the three dialog windows use solid `#1c1f26` backgrounds that clash with the main app's liquid glass design. The goal is to make every part of the settings UI match the glassmorphism aesthetic from `glass.css`.

## Approach

Add CSS classes to `glass.css`, then update the Java code to use them — replacing inline styles and the solid dialog backgrounds with glassmorphism.

---

## Step 1: Add CSS classes to `glass.css`

Append these classes after the `.section-title` block (after line 396):

- **`.glass-dialog`** — frosted glass panel for dialog `Stage` roots: `rgba(13,14,17,0.85)` bg, `rgba(255,255,255,0.10)` border, 12px radius, drop shadow. More opaque than the main window pane since dialogs have no orb layer behind them.
- **`.glass-field` + `.glass-field:focused`** — form inputs with accent glow on focus (matches search-bar pattern)
- **`.glass-field-label`** — 11px/`0.50` alpha label for form field labels
- **`.section-header`** — 15px/`0.92` alpha for larger section titles (distinct from existing `.section-title` at `0.28`)
- **`.glass-combo`** — ComboBox glass styling
- **`.glass-table`** — TableView with glass header, accent-colored selection rows, hover states, transparent placeholder
- **`.glass-checkbox`** — CheckBox with glass box styling and accent selected state
- **`.glass-btn-primary`** — accent-filled button with hover glow + lift
- **`.glass-btn-secondary`** — ghost button with hover state

## Step 2: Update UI helper methods

In `SwissKitJSettingUi.java` lines 937-1016, update the factory methods to use CSS classes:

1. **`glassBtn()`** (line 992) — use `glass-btn-primary` / `glass-btn-secondary` classes instead of inline strings
2. **`fieldStyle()`** (line 977) — use `glass-field` class
3. **`comboStyle()`** (line 985) — use `glass-combo` class
4. **`sectionTitle()`** (line 952) — use `section-header` class
5. **`subLabel()`** (line 958) — use `glass-field-label` class
6. **`labeled()`** (line 941) — use `glass-field-label` class for the label
7. **`textField()`** (line 964) — apply `glass-field` class when no custom style
8. **`passwordField()`** (line 971) — apply `glass-field` class

## Step 3: Fix dialog windows (core change)

Each of the 3 dialogs needs its root VBox background changed and scene fill set to dark:

### Address Book dialog (line ~403)
- Line 465: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: #1c1f26;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-dialog")`
- Line 418: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: transparent;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-table")` on the TableView
- After scene creation: set scene fill to `#0d0e11` so glass-dialog blends

### Add/Edit Address dialog (line ~476)
- Line 599: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: #1c1f26;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-dialog")`
- After scene creation: set scene fill to `#0d0e11`
- Text fields and tagCombo automatically benefit from Step 2 helper changes

### Manage Tags dialog (line ~610)
- Line 708: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: #1c1f26;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-dialog")`
- Line 625: replace `setStyle("-fx-background-color: transparent;")` with `getStyleClass().add("glass-table")`
- After scene creation: set scene fill to `#0d0e11`

## Step 4: Polish main settings container

- Line 72-79: add `glass-dialog` class, simplify inline style
- Line 729: plugin TableView — use `glass-table` class
- Lines 293-297: TLS/SSL checkboxes — use `glass-checkbox` class

## Step 5: Build verification

Run Maven build to verify no compilation errors:
```bash
mvn compile -pl SwissKit -am -DskipTests
```

If the build succeeds, the changes are structurally correct.

---

## Files modified

1. `SwissKit/src/main/resources/css/glass.css` — append 8 new CSS class blocks
2. `SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/ui/setting/SwissKitJSettingUi.java` — update helpers (lines 937-1016), dialogs (lines 403-717), container (lines 70-143)

content.content:
1	# SwissKit
2	
3	![SwissKit](https://img.shields.io/badge/SwissKit-Desktop%20Toolbox-blue) ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-11-orange) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green) ![Maven](https://img.shields.io/badge/Maven-3.6+-red)
4	
5	**SwissKit** is a *modular desktop toolbox* built with Java Swing. It provides a clean, extensible platform for various
6	utility tools including Excel file processing, email sending, email address book management, and plugin support. The
7	application uses a plugin-based architecture with automatic page discovery, making it easy to add new functionality.
8	
9	If you want a lightweight, fast, and customizable desktop utility suite, this is it.
10	
11	---
12	
13	## Quick Start
14	
15	**Requirements:**
16	
17	- **JDK 11 or higher**
18	- **Maven 3.6 or higher**
19	
20	### Installation
21	
22	```bash
23	# Clone the repository
24	git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
25	cd SwissKitJ
26	
27	# Install API module first (required)
28	mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests
29	
30	# Build the main project
31	mvn clean package
32	
33	# Run the application (executable JAR)
34	java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
35	```
36	
37	Or using Maven exec plugin:
38	
39	```bash
40	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
41	```
42	
43	### Development Mode
44	
45	```bash
46	# Compile
47	mvn clean compile
48	
49	# Run
50	mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
51	```
52	
53	---
54	
55	## Features
56	
57	- **📦 Modular Architecture** - Plugin-based design with SPI auto-discovery
58	- **🎨 Modern UI** - Built with Swing and FlatLaf theme framework
59	- **⚡ High Performance** - Uses Apache FESOD for efficient Excel processing
60	- **🔄 Async Processing** - Background tasks with SwingWorker for non-blocking UI
61	- **💾 Database Support** - H2 + MyBatis for persistent storage
62	- **📧 Email Management** - Address book with tags for contact organization
63	- **🔌 Plugin System** - Install external JAR plugins to extend functionality
64	- **🛠️ Easy Extension** - Add new tools by implementing the `KitPage` interface
65	- **📊 Custom UI Components** - Gradient progress bar, fixed-width combo box
66	- **🚀 Splash Screen** - Professional startup experience with loading indicator
67	
68	### Current Tools
69	
70	#### 📊 Excel Tool
71	
72	- **File Analysis** - Read Excel file structure and extract headers
73	- **File Splitting** - Split Excel files by sheets or column values
74	- **Complex Split Mode** - Advanced splitting with custom configuration
75	- **Config View** - View and edit split configurations
76	- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress updates with percentage display
77	- **Multi-Sheet Support** - Handle multiple sheets in a single file
78	- **Streaming Data Processing** - Efficient memory usage with Apache FESOD
79	
80	#### 📧 Email Tool
81	
82	- **Email Composition** - Compose emails with subject and body
83	- **Recipient Management** - Add multiple recipients (To, Cc)
84	- **Mass Email Support** - Send emails to multiple contacts based on tags
85	- **Tag-based Recipients** - Load recipients from address book by tags
86	- **Attachment by Tag** - Attach files from tag-based folder selection
87	- **SMTP Integration** - Full SMTP support with TLS/SSL

file.content:
# SwissKit

![SwissKit](https://img.shields.io/badge/SwissKit-Desktop%20Toolbox-blue) ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-11-orange) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green) ![Maven](https://img.shields.io/badge/Maven-3.6+-red)

**SwissKit** is a *modular desktop toolbox* built with Java Swing. It provides a clean, extensible platform for various
utility tools including Excel file processing, email sending, email address book management, and plugin support. The
application uses a plugin-based architecture with automatic page discovery, making it easy to add new functionality.

If you want a lightweight, fast, and customizable desktop utility suite, this is it.

---

## Quick Start

**Requirements:**

- **JDK 11 or higher**
- **Maven 3.6 or higher**

### Installation

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the main project
mvn clean package

# Run the application (executable JAR)
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

Or using Maven exec plugin:

```bash
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

### Development Mode

```bash
# Compile
mvn clean compile

# Run
mvn exec:java -Dexec.mainClass="fan.summer.Main"
```

---

## Features

- **📦 Modular Architecture** - Plugin-based design with SPI auto-discovery
- **🎨 Modern UI** - Built with Swing and FlatLaf theme framework
- **⚡ High Performance** - Uses Apache FESOD for efficient Excel processing
- **🔄 Async Processing** - Background tasks with SwingWorker for non-blocking UI
- **💾 Database Support** - H2 + MyBatis for persistent storage
- **📧 Email Management** - Address book with tags for contact organization
- **🔌 Plugin System** - Install external JAR plugins to extend functionality
- **🛠️ Easy Extension** - Add new tools by implementing the `KitPage` interface
- **📊 Custom UI Components** - Gradient progress bar, fixed-width combo box
- **🚀 Splash Screen** - Professional startup experience with loading indicator

### Current Tools

#### 📊 Excel Tool

- **File Analysis** - Read Excel file structure and extract headers
- **File Splitting** - Split Excel files by sheets or column values
- **Complex Split Mode** - Advanced splitting with custom configuration
- **Config View** - View and edit split configurations
- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress updates with percentage display
- **Multi-Sheet Support** - Handle multiple sheets in a single file
- **Streaming Data Processing** - Efficient memory usage with Apache FESOD

#### 📧 Email Tool

- **Email Composition** - Compose emails with subject and body
- **Recipient Management** - Add multiple recipients (To, Cc)
- **Mass Email Support** - Send emails to multiple contacts based on tags
- **Tag-based Recipients** - Load recipients from address book by tags
- **Attachment by Tag** - Attach files from tag-based folder selection
- **SMTP Integration** - Full SMTP support with TLS/SSL
- **Sent Log Viewer** - View history of sent emails with status tracking

#### ⚙️ Settings

- **Email Server Configuration** - SMTP settings with TLS/SSL support
- **Email Address Book** - Manage email contacts with nicknames; double-click to edit
- **Tag Management** - Create and manage tags for categorizing contacts
- **Plugin Installation** - Install JAR plugins via file upload

#### 🏠 Welcome Page

- Application overview and guidance
- Quick access to all tools

#### 🎓 HappyLearning Plugin

- **Auto Learning** - Automated online learning with configurable passkey
- **Config Upload** - Upload and manage learning configuration files
- **Progress Tracking** - Real-time progress bars for major and elective subjects
- **Start/Stop Control** - Manual control over learning sessions
- **Background Processing** - Non-blocking UI with SwingWorker

---

## Architecture

### Project Modules

SwissKit uses a multi-module Maven structure:

| Module                 | Description                                                             |
|------------------------|-------------------------------------------------------------------------|
| `SwissKitJ-Api`        | Shared API module containing interfaces, annotations, and UI components |
| `SwissKit` (main)      | Core application with Excel, Email, and Settings tools                  |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning` | Auto-learning plugin with progress tracking     |

### Project Structure

```
SwissKitJ/                          # Project root
├── SwissKitJ-Api/                  # Shared API module
│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│       ├── annoattion/
│       │   └── SwissKitPage.java
│       ├── api/
│       │   └── KitPage.java
│       └── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/components/
│           ├── GradientProgressBar.java
│           └── FixedWidthComboBox.java
├── SwissKit/                       # Main application
│   └── src/main/java/fan/summer/
│       ├── Main.java
│       ├── database/               # H2 + MyBatis
│       ├── kitpage/                # Excel, Email, Settings pages
│       ├── plugin/                 # PluginLoader, IsolatedPluginClassLoader
│       ├── scaner/                 # SwissKitPageScaner (SPI)
│       ├── plugin.swisskit.hpl.ui/ # UI components
│       └── utils/                  # ExcelUtil, EmailUtil, etc.
├── OfficalPlugin/
│   └── SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning/  # Built-in plugin
└── docs/                           # Documentation
```

### Plugin System

SwissKit uses an automatic discovery mechanism with annotations and SPI (Service Provider Interface) for tool pages:

1. **Interface**: Implement the `KitPage` interface (from `fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
2. **Annotation**: Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation (from `fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage`)
3. **SPI Registration**: Add class name to `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
4. **Auto-discovery**: Pages are automatically discovered at runtime using `SwissKitPageScaner`
5. **Sorting**: Pages are sorted by `order()` value in annotation

Example:

```java
package fan.summer.kitpage.mytool;

import fan.summer.api.KitPage;
import fan.summer.annoattion.SwissKitPage;

import javax.swing.*;

@SwissKitPage(
        menuName = "🔧 My Tool",
        menuTooltip = "Open My Tool",
        visible = true,
        order = 10
)
public class MyToolPage implements KitPage {
  private JPanel panel;

  public MyToolPage() {
    initComponents();
  }

  private void initComponents() {
    panel = new JPanel(new BorderLayout());
    JLabel titleLabel = new JLabel("My Tool");
    titleLabel.setFont(new Font("SansSerif", Font.BOLD, 18));
    panel.add(titleLabel, BorderLayout.NORTH);
  }

  @Override
  public JPanel getPanel() {
    return panel;
  }
}
```

### Tech Stack

| Category                | Technology       | Version          |
|-------------------------|------------------|------------------|
| **Language**            | Java             | 11               |
| **Build Tool**          | Maven            | 3.6+             |
| **UI Framework**        | Swing + FlatLaf  | 3.5              |
| **Excel Processing**    | Apache FESOD     | 2.0.1-incubating |
| **Database**            | H2 + MyBatis     | 2.4.240 / 3.5.19 |
| **Logging**             | Log4j2 + SLF4J   | 2.25.3 / 2.0.16  |
| **JSON**                | FastJSON2        | 2.0.59           |
| **Code Simplification** | Lombok           | 1.18.42          |
| **Email**               | Simple Java Mail | 8.12.6           |

---

## Database

### Tables

| Table Name                | Purpose                                    |
|---------------------------|--------------------------------------------|
| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration                   |
| `complex_split_config`    | Excel complex split configuration          |
| `email_address_book`      | Email contacts with nicknames and tags     |
| `email_tag`               | Tags for categorizing email contacts       |
| `email_mass_sent_config`  | Mass email sending configuration           |
| `email_sent_log`          | Email sending history with status tracking |

### Database Location

- **Path**: `.swisskit/swisskit.db` (relative to application runtime directory)
- **Type**: H2 (embedded, file-based, no external server required)

---

## Development

### Adding a New Tool

1. Create a new package under `fan.summer.kitpage` (e.g., `pdf/`, `image/`)
2. Create a class implementing `KitPage` (from `SwissKitJ-Api` module)
3. Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation for menu configuration
4. Register in SPI plugin.swisskit.hpl.service file (`META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`)
5. The tool will be automatically discovered

### Creating a Plugin

1. Create a new Maven project with `SwissKitJ-Api` as dependency:
   ```xml
   <dependency>
       <groupId>fan.summer.api</groupId>
       <artifactId>SwissKitJ-Api</artifactId>
       <version>2.0.1</version>
   </dependency>
   ```
2. Implement `KitPage` interface
3. Add `@SwissKitPage` annotation
4. Register in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.KitPage`
5. Package as JAR and install via Settings page

### Code Style

- Use camelCase for method and variable names
- Use PascalCase for class names
- Add Javadoc comments for all public methods
- All UI text and comments should be in English
- Use SLF4J Logger for logging
- Use `SansSerif` font for UI components

### Building

```bash
# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Clean build with executable JAR
mvn clean package

# Skip tests
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run executable JAR
java -jar target/SwissKit-2.1.1.jar
```

---

## Roadmap

- [x] Excel file analysis functionality
- [x] Excel file split by sheet functionality
- [x] Excel file split by column functionality
- [x] Excel complex split mode with configuration
- [x] Email address book management
- [x] Email tag management
- [x] Plugin installation support
- [x] Email sending with SMTP support
- [x] Mass email sending with tag-based recipients
- [x] Email sent log viewing functionality
- [ ] Add PDF processing tool
- [ ] Add image processing tool
- [x] Support theme switching
- [ ] Add unit tests
- [x] Add multi-language support

---

## Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please follow these guidelines:

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature`)
3. Commit your changes (`git commit -m ':sparkles: Add some AmazingFeature'`)
4. Push to the branch (`git push origin feature/AmazingFeature`)
5. Open a Pull Request

### Commit Message Format

Follow the conventional commits format with emojis:

- `✨` / `:sparkles:` - New feature
- `📝` / `:memo:` - Documentation
- `🐛` / `:bug:` - Bug fix
- `♻️` / `:recycle:` - Refactor code
- `⬆️` / `:arrow_up:` - Upgrade dependency

---

## License

This project is licensed under the MIT License - see the LICENSE file for details.

---

## Authors

- **Summer** - Core development
- **PhoebeJ** - Email & Excel functionality

---

## Documentation

For detailed technical documentation, see [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md).

For user documentation, see the [docs](docs/) folder.

---

**Built with ❤️ using Java and Swing**

1	# Changelog
2	
3	All notable changes to SwissKit will be documented in this file.
4	
5	The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
6	and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).
7	
8	---
9	
10	## [Unreleased]
11	
12	### 🐛 Bug Fixes
13	
14	- **Plugin Loading**: Handle plugin loading errors gracefully to ensure app startup when corrupted plugins exist
15	
16	---
17	
18	## 1.1.0
19	
20	**Release Date:** 2026-03-30
21	
22	---
23	
24	### ✨ New Features
25	
26	- **Plugin Hot-Deployment**: Deploy, reload, and uninstall plugins without restarting the application
27	- **HappyLearning Enhancement**: Add class hours tracking and status display
28

The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/en/1.0.0/),
and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/spec/v2.0.0.html).

---

## [Unreleased]

- **Plugin Loading**: Handle plugin loading errors gracefully to ensure app startup when corrupted plugins exist

---

## 1.1.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-30

---

### ✨ New Features

- **Plugin Hot-Deployment**: Deploy, reload, and uninstall plugins without restarting the application
- **HappyLearning Enhancement**: Add class hours tracking and status display

### 🔄 Changes

- **Project Restructuring**: Renamed `Happy-learning` → `SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning`, moved plugins to `OfficalPlugin/` directory
- **Module Organization**: Added `SwissKitJ-Api` as a shared API module

---

## 1.0.0

**Release Date:** 2026-03-29

---

input.content:
# SwissKit

![SwissKit](https://img.shields.io/badge/SwissKit-Desktop%20Toolbox-blue) ![Java](https://img.shields.io/badge/Java-17-orange) ![License](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-green) ![Maven](https://img.shields.io/badge/Maven-3.6+-red)

**SwissKit** is a *modular desktop toolbox* built with JavaFX. It provides a clean, extensible platform for various
utility tools including Excel file processing, email sending, and plugin support. The application uses a
plugin-based architecture with automatic service discovery, making it easy to add new functionality.

If you want a lightweight, fast, and customizable desktop utility suite with a modern glassmorphism UI, this is it.

---

## Quick Start

**Requirements:**

- **JDK 17 or higher** (recommended: [Eclipse Temurin](https://adoptium.net/))
- **Maven 3.6 or higher**

### Installation

```bash
# Clone the repository
git clone https://github.com/MuskStark/SwissKitJ.git
cd SwissKitJ

# Install API module first (required)
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build the main project
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run the application
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-3.0.0.jar
```

### Platform-Specific Builds

The GitHub Actions workflow builds three platform packages:

| Platform | File | How to run |
|---|---|---|
| Windows 10/11 (64-bit) | `SwissKit-3.0.0-windows.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.exe` |
| Linux x64 | `SwissKit-3.0.0-linux.zip` | Extract then run `./run.sh` |
| macOS Apple Silicon (M1/M2/M3) | `SwissKit-3.0.0-macos-apple-silicon.zip` | Extract then double-click `SwissKit.app` |

All platforms require Java 11+ installed on the target machine.

---

## Features

- **🎨 Glassmorphism UI** — Modern JavaFX UI with frosted glass effects, animated sidebar, and glowing accents
- **📦 Modular Architecture** — Plugin-based design with Java ServiceLoader auto-discovery
- **⚡ High Performance** — Uses Apache FESOD for efficient Excel processing
- **🔌 Plugin Store** — Browse and install plugins from the online store with one-click installation
- **💾 Database Support** — H2 + MyBatis for persistent storage
- **📧 Email Management** — Address book with tags for contact organization, mass sending
- **🛠️ Easy Extension** — Add new tools by implementing the `SwissKitJPlugin` interface

### Built-in Tools

#### 📊 Excel Splitter

- **4-Step Wizard** — Guided workflow: Select file → Choose split mode → Configure → Output
- **Split by Sheet** — One output file per selected sheet
- **Split by Column** — Group rows by unique column value
- **Complex Split Mode** — Multi-config splitting with saved configurations
- **Progress Tracking** — Real-time progress with streaming (low memory usage)
- **Multi-Sheet Support** — Handle multiple sheets in a single file

#### 📧 Email

- **Email Composition** — Subject and body with plain-text/HTML toggle
- **Recipient Management** — Add multiple recipients, CC/BCC support
- **Mass Email** — Send to contacts filtered by tags
- **Attachment by Tag** — Attach files from tag-based folder selection
- **SMTP Integration** — Full SMTP support with TLS/SSL
- **Sent Log** — View history of sent emails with status tracking

#### ⚙️ Settings

- **Email Server** — SMTP configuration with TLS/SSL
- **Address Book** — Manage contacts with nicknames; double-click to edit
- **Tag Management** — Create and manage tags for contacts
- **Plugin Store URL** — Configure the online plugin store endpoint

---

## Architecture

### Project Modules

SwissKit uses a multi-module Maven structure:

| Module | Description |
|--------|-------------|
| `SwissKitJ-Api` | Shared plugin interface + reusable UI components (`SwissKitJPlugin`, `StepWizard`) |
| `SwissKit` | Main JavaFX application — UI shell, plugin loading, built-in tools |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-HappyLearning` | Auto-learning plugin |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKitJ-Plugin-Qcc` | CSV-to-Excel converter plugin |
| `OfficalPlugin/SwissKit-Plugin-Mouse` | Mouse automation plugin |

### UI Structure

```
MainWindow (StageStyle.TRANSPARENT)
├── TitleBar           — Custom window chrome (drag, minimize, maximize, close)
├── Sidebar            — Category navigation (all / text / image / dev / net / other)
├── ContentArea        — ToolCard grid or active tool view
└── DetailPanel        — Slide-in panel with plugin metadata + Launch button
```

### Plugin System

Plugins implement `fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin` (from `SwissKitJ-Api`):

```java
public interface SwissKitJPlugin {
    String getId();          // reverse-domain ID, e.g. "com.example.my-tool"
    String getName();
    String getDescription();
    String getCategory();    // dev / text / image / net / other
    String getVersion();
    String getIconText();    // emoji or single char
    Node createView();       // JavaFX Node — cached and reused

    default void onActivate()   {}
    default void onDeactivate() {}
    default void onUnload()     {}
}
```

Register via `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin`, then drop the JAR into the `plugins/` directory. Hot-reload is supported.

### Built-in Tool Registration

Built-in tools (those packaged with the main app) bypass SPI and are registered directly via `BuiltinToolRegistrar`:

```java
BuiltinToolRegistrar.register(builtinPluginInstance);
```

---

## Tech Stack

| Category | Technology | Version |
|---|---|---|
| **Language** | Java | 17 (target) / 11 (minimum) |
| **Build Tool** | Maven | 3.6+ |
| **UI Framework** | JavaFX | 21 |
| **Theming** | Custom CSS (glassmorphism) | — |
| **Excel Processing** | Apache FESOD | 2.0.1-incubating |
| **Database** | H2 + MyBatis | 2.4.240 / 3.5.19 |
| **Logging** | SLF4J + Logback | 2.0.16 / 1.3.15 |
| **Email** | Simple Java Mail | 8.12.6 |

---

## Development

### Adding a Built-in Tool

1. Create a class implementing `SwissKitJPlugin` in `SwissKit/src/main/java/fan/summer/kitpage/`
2. Register it in `BuiltinToolRegistrar.register()` during app startup
3. The tool will appear in the sidebar under its category

### Creating an External Plugin

1. Create a Maven project with `SwissKitJ-Api` as a `provided` dependency
2. Implement `SwissKitJPlugin`
3. Register in `META-INF/services/fan.summer.api.SwissKitJPlugin`
4. Package as JAR and install via the Plugin Store or Local Install tab

### Building

```bash
# Install API module first
mvn install -f SwissKitJ-Api/pom.xml -DskipTests

# Build all modules
mvn clean package -DskipTests

# Run
java -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-3.0.0.jar
```

### Running with Local Plugin Store

Override the store URL for local testing:

```bash
java -Dstore.url=http://localhost:8888/plugins/store.json -jar SwissKit/target/SwissKit-3.0.0.jar
```

---

## Database

### Tables

| Table | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `app_setting` | General app settings (store URL, etc.) |
| `swiss_kit_setting_email` | Email SMTP configuration |
| `complex_split_config` | Excel complex split configurations |
| `email_address_book` | Email contacts with nicknames and tags |
| `email_tag` | Tags for categorizing contacts |
| `email_mass_sent_config` | Mass email sending configuration |
| `email_sent_log` | Email sending history |

### Location

`.swisskit/swisskit.db` relative to the application runtime directory. H2 embedded file-based — no external server required.

---

## Roadmap

- [x] Excel file analysis and split by sheet
- [x] Excel split by column value
- [x] Excel complex split mode
- [x] Email address book management
- [x] Email tag management
- [x] Plugin installation (online + local)
- [x] Email sending with SMTP (TLS/SSL)
- [x] Mass email with tag-based recipients
- [x] Email sent log viewing
- [x] JavaFX UI redesign (glassmorphism)
- [x] Plugin Store with online install
- [ ] PDF processing tool
- [ ] Image processing tool
- [ ] Add unit tests
- [ ] Theme switching (light/dark)

---

## Contributing

1. Fork the repository
2. Create a feature branch (`git checkout -b feature/AmazingFeature`)
3. Commit your changes using conventional commits with emojis
4. Push to the branch
5. Open a Pull Request

### Commit Message Format

- `✨` — New feature
- `📝` — Documentation
- `🐛` — Bug fix
- `♻️` — Refactor
- `⬆️` — Dependency upgrade

---

## License

MIT License — see [LICENSE](LICENSE) file.

---

## Documentation

- [CHANGELOG](CHANGELOG.md) — Release history
- [AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md) — Technical documentation for AI assistants
- Online docs: https://muskstark.github.io/SwissKitJ/

---

**Built with ❤️ using JavaFX**
